title: Adlib Inc. 1991 Gold 1000 Retro Sound Card ISA
summary: This forum post discusses the sale of an Adlib Inc. 1991 Gold 1000 Retro Sound Card ISA. Users explain its high value due to its rarity, nostalgic appeal, and desirability for retro computer builds among collectors. The card is listed on eBay, and the price reflects its sought-after status.

title: Is eBay's Restatement Of GMV Really Immaterial?
summary: eBay recently changed its definition of Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) to include shipping fees and taxes, claiming the impact on previously reported figures is "immaterial." However, an analysis of the restated historical metrics reveals a significant annual adjustment of approximately $13 billion, totaling nearly $40 billion over several years, casting doubt on eBay's "immaterial" claim. The article also highlights how this change affects the definition of "Active Buyers" and raises questions about how sales tax and shipping were previously accounted for.

title: eBay Named as a Best Place to Work in 2022 by Glassdoor
summary: eBay has been honored with an 2022 Employees’ Choice Award by Glassdoor, recognizing it as a best place to work among U.S. companies with more than 1,000 employees. This award is based on employee reviews gathered between October 2020 and October 2021, evaluating factors such as career opportunities, compensation, culture, diversity, senior management, and work/life balance. The company's Chief People Officer, Cornelius Boone, expressed pride in the award, emphasizing eBay's commitment to supporting and valuing its employees.

title: Lightsource bp closes financing package and mobilizes construction on Louisiana’s largest solar project
summary: Lightsource bp has secured a $533 million multi-project financing package and commenced construction on the 345-megawatt Ventress Solar project in Louisiana, which will be the state's largest solar facility. The project is backed by long-term power purchase agreements with McDonald’s Corporation and eBay Inc. This initiative is expected to create approximately 400 construction jobs and provide significant economic benefits to Pointe Coupee Parish.

title: Lightsource bp closes on $533 million project package in Louisiana
summary: Lightsource bp has finalized a $533 million multi-project financing package to fund its 345 MW Ventress Solar project in Louisiana and other solar initiatives. The Ventress project will supply clean energy to McDonald’s Corporation and eBay Inc. under long-term power purchase agreements and is expected to create 400 construction jobs and contribute $30 million to Pointe Coupee Parish. Construction has begun, with full commercial operation anticipated in late 2023.

title: Gen X (Remember Them?) Are Driving the Secondhand Market, The RealReal Says
summary: The RealReal's latest report indicates that Generation X is the primary driver of the secondhand luxury market, even as younger generations show increasing interest in sustainable shopping. The report highlights that a commitment to sustainability is attracting shoppers to resale platforms, with a significant number replacing fast fashion purchases and prioritizing secondhand items for their eco-friendliness. While luxury brands like Gucci and Louis Vuitton are top sellers, there's a notable rise in demand for vintage, unique pieces, and gender-fluid styles across all demographics.

title: StubHub Explores Going Public, Valued at $13B
summary: StubHub Holdings is considering a direct listing that could value the company at over $13 billion, following other ticket platforms going public. The parent company of StubHub and Viagogo has filed paperwork with the SEC and is working with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase. Despite facing scrutiny over its merger and carrying significant debt, StubHub expects to rebound to pre-pandemic revenue levels in 2022, though the Omicron variant poses a potential challenge.

title: StubHub Reportedly Considers Going Public
summary: StubHub Holdings, which operates ticket resale platforms StubHub and viagogo, is reportedly exploring going public via a direct listing as early as 2022. This move could value the company at over $13 billion, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. advising. The decision to pursue a direct listing means selling existing shares without issuing new ones, distinguishing it from a traditional IPO.

title: Artificial Intelligence at PayPal – Two Unique Use-Cases
summary: PayPal leverages AI and machine learning for two critical business functions: enhancing payment authorization rates and preventing fraud. By using ML models like Gradient Boosting Machines, PayPal predicts and remedies issuer declines, significantly increasing approval rates for valid transactions. For fraud prevention, the company employs machine learning and graph technologies to analyze vast transaction data in real-time, identifying suspicious patterns and reducing financial losses for merchants and buyers.

title: eBay Launches Authentication for Trading Cards
summary: eBay has launched its Authenticity Guarantee service for trading cards, initially covering single ungraded cards sold for $750+ in the U.S., with plans to expand to graded, autograph, and patch cards. This service aims to increase buyer and seller confidence in the rapidly growing trading card market, which saw $2 billion in transactions in the first half of 2021. The authentication process involves a multi-point physical inspection by third-party authenticators, contributing to eBay's commitment to trusted marketplaces for high-value items.

title: eBay Inc. stock falls Thursday, underperforms market
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock fell by 1.75% on Thursday, closing at $56.70. This decline occurred during a generally poor trading session for the market, with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also seeing decreases. The stock underperformed compared to some competitors and closed significantly below its 52-week high.

title: eBay Video In Listings Delays Raise Server Processing & Latency Concerns
summary: eBay is facing significant delays in processing videos uploaded by sellers to their listings, leading to concerns about server capacity and artificial latency. Sellers report videos remaining "under review" for days, impacting sales, while eBay blames backlogs, raising questions about their review process for this new feature. The article suggests these delays might be an intentional part of eBay's "Speed" initiative from 2019, prioritizing buyer-facing site performance over seller tools during peak times.

title: Power battle at Huntsman heats up, with activist investor Starboard raising the stakes
summary: A proxy battle is intensifying at Houston-area chemical company Huntsman Corp., with activist investor Starboard Value pushing for changes to the board of directors. Starboard, which owns nearly 9% of Huntsman, criticizes the company's underperformance and missed financial targets, while Huntsman defends its ongoing initiatives to create shareholder value. This contest is not the first for Huntsman and reflects broader boardroom activism within the chemicals sector, with analysts noting Huntsman has lagged some peers in efficiency.

title: PayPal scraps account growth goal
summary: PayPal has abandoned its goal of reaching 750 million customer accounts by 2025 following disappointing fourth-quarter results and a lowered revenue growth forecast for 2022. The company now plans to shift its strategy away from aggressive new user acquisition to focus more on engaging existing account holders and boosting their activity, aiming for sustainable growth and higher-value customers. This strategic pivot comes amidst macroeconomic pressures and the lingering impact of its separation from eBay.

title: How Malaysia Got in on the Secondhand Clothing Boom (Published 2022)
summary: Malaysia has become a significant player in the booming global secondhand clothing market, with many sellers operating from the country on platforms like Etsy, eBay, and Grailed. This trend is driven by increased consumer interest in affordable, eco-friendly alternatives to fast fashion, fueled by factors such as the pandemic and environmental concerns. The secondhand clothing sector is projected to reach nearly $77 billion by 2025.

title: MySize Acquires Orgad, an Omnichannel E-Commerce Platform with a Presence on Amazon.com
summary: MySize, Inc. has acquired Orgad, an omnichannel e-commerce platform with global operations, including on Amazon.com. This strategic acquisition expands MySize's business into direct e-commerce selling and is expected to leverage MySizeID technology to increase profitability by reducing returns. Orgad generated approximately $2.5 million in revenue in 2021 and manages over 1,000 SKUs, primarily in fashion and apparel.

title: Inside the $644 billion business of reselling returned items, from electronics to bizarre finds
summary: The liquidation market, which resells returned goods, has grown into a $644 billion industry due to the increasing volume of returns, particularly from online shopping. This sector offers a solution for retailers facing significant costs and environmental impact from returns, with companies like Liquidity Services and B-Stock Solutions specializing in reselling everything from electronics to unusual items. The boom in liquidation is also fueled by growing consumer demand for sustainable and secondhand goods, leading to the rise of direct-to-consumer liquidation stores and refurbished item marketplaces.

title: Most Notable Earnings for the Week Starting Feb 21, 2022
summary: This article outlines the highly anticipated earnings releases for the week beginning February 21, 2022, featuring major companies like EBAY, BABA, and MRNA. It provides a daily breakdown of companies reporting, their respective sectors, and earnings call timings, serving as a guide for investors during earnings season. The piece also suggests using TipRanks' tools for deeper analysis into these stocks.

title: E-commerce: EBay forecasts bleak quarter as pandemic-driven online demand wanes
summary: EBay Inc. forecasts a challenging first quarter with lower-than-expected profits and revenue due to waning online demand, increased competition, and supply chain issues. The company's shares fell significantly as the pandemic-driven e-commerce boom subsides and consumers return to in-person shopping, mirroring a trend also seen by Amazon. Despite this, eBay's fourth-quarter results exceeded analyst estimates for profit and revenue, though gross merchandise volume and active buyers declined.

title: Alibaba Plans for New Normal of Low Growth as Crackdown Bites
summary: Alibaba is shifting its strategy to focus on user retention rather than aggressive market-share expansion, following over a year of regulatory crackdowns in China's internet sector. This change comes as the company reported its slowest revenue growth since its IPO, highlighting the significant financial impact of Beijing's policies on its technology sector. CEO Daniel Zhang outlined this new approach, prioritizing existing users over new acquisitions.

title: Celebrating eBay’s Women Sellers
summary: In celebration of International Women's Day and Women’s History Month, eBay highlights inspiring female sellers on its platform. The article shares stories of women entrepreneurs from the UK, Indonesia, and the US who have built successful businesses selling diverse products, from African-inspired jewelry to custom motorcycle helmets and beauty products. These stories demonstrate how eBay empowers women to achieve their financial goals and pursue their passions.

title: Traction on Demand’s Greg Malpass on the Salesforce offer he couldn’t refuse
summary: Greg Malpass, founder and CEO of Traction on Demand, sold his company to Salesforce after 15 years, despite previously resisting acquisition offers. The deal, which has an undisclosed sale price but is expected to generate US$75 million in revenue for Salesforce in 2023, was an "incredible opportunity" that Malpass felt accountable to his employees to pursue. Traction on Demand, a Salesforce partner with 1,500 global employees, will become part of Salesforce Professional Services, with Malpass focused on a smooth integration before exploring his next steps within the larger company.

title: eBay Hosts Investor Day, Outlines Long-Term Strategy to Build eBay for the Future
summary: eBay Inc. hosted its first Investor Day under CEO Jamie Iannone, detailing its long-term growth strategy and new initiatives. The company previewed the "eBay Vault" for secure storage and trading of collectibles and luxury goods, and introduced its first digital wallet for customer convenience. eBay also provided a business outlook with guidance for 2022 and projections for 2023 and 2024, emphasizing a tech-led reimagination of the platform.

title: eBay expects revenue growth of up to 6% in 2023, launches digital wallet
summary: E-commerce company eBay (EBAY.O) anticipates revenue growth of 5% to 6% in fiscal 2023 and introduced its first digital wallet to allow customers to use sales proceeds for purchases or expenses. The company also launched eBay Vault for trading cards and collectibles, despite facing stiff competition from rivals like Amazon and waning online demand. eBay forecasts higher revenue and adjusted profit growth for fiscal 2024.

title: eBay To Launch its Own Digital Wallet and other Digital Transactions News briefs from 3/11/22
summary: eBay announced the launch of its own digital wallet to allow sellers to use sales proceeds for purchases or expenses. Western Union, Discover, and Cleverbridge have suspended operations in Russia and Belarus following the invasion of Ukraine. New data also shows an increase in Visa spending momentum and the growing adoption of Buy Now, Pay Later options.

title: Russia attacks Ukraine: eBay blocking all transactions involving addresses in Russia
summary: Online auction site eBay has temporarily halted all transactions involving Russian addresses and some transactions involving Ukrainian addresses due to service interruptions by payment vendors and shipping carriers. This action aligns with other major companies suspending operations in Russia following its invasion of Ukraine. eBay is also supporting Ukrainian sellers by waiving fees and protecting them from penalties, and allowing customers to donate to relief organizations.

title: San Jose-Based EBay Suspends Transactions to Russian Addresses
summary: EBay, the San Jose-based e-commerce giant, has temporarily suspended all transactions involving Russian addresses due to service interruptions from payment vendors and shipping carriers. The company stated its support for Ukraine, waiving seller fees, protecting sellers from late shipment penalties, and matching employee donations to Ukrainian support organizations. Additionally, eBay has removed all products promoting Vladimir Putin, allowing only historical or educational related materials.

title: eBay Ventures Offers Venture Capital To Innovative Founders
summary: eBay Ventures, the venture capital arm of eBay Inc., offers funding and strategic expertise to innovative founders in areas adjacent to eBay's core business. The firm has made its first investment in Norwegian second-hand marketplace Tise, backing it in a $20 million funding round. eBay Ventures aims to support startups that can bring new value, expand business, and provide a competitive advantage, reflecting eBay's "build, buy, or partner" strategy.

title: eBay Wallet & New Payments Features
summary: eBay announced new payment features, including a digital wallet launching in Q2 2022 to allow "spendable funds" for purchases and fees, replacing functions previously offered by PayPal. The company is also rolling out additional payout schedules, exploring working capital financial products, and partnering with Chase Freedom for cashback. Additionally, eBay Ventures has been revealed as a new multi-stage investment firm focusing on e-commerce startups.

title: EBay creates digital wallet, plans storage vault for collectibles
summary: EBay announced the launch of its first digital wallet for users to manage sales revenue and a forthcoming EBay Vault, a storage facility for trading cards and other collectibles, with plans to expand to luxury goods. These initiatives aim to enhance trust in the marketplace and expand payment capabilities, building on prior authentication efforts for high-value items. The company expects the EBay Vault to secure up to $3 billion in assets, positioning it as a significant global non-governmental asset warehouse, and is also addressing counterfeiting concerns with increased seller transparency.

title: Tradesy online fashion marketplace acquired
summary: The online fashion marketplace Tradesy has been acquired, a move that highlights a shared ambition to accelerate the growth of the circular fashion economy. This acquisition comes as the global market value for secondhand and resale apparel was estimated at $36 billion in 2021.

title: Update: $12,550 manmade diamond sale to benefit thousands of Americans in need
summary: The article details the sale of "The Ranch Diamond," a lab-grown diamond made from Hidden Valley Ranch Seasoning, which fetched $12,550 on eBay. All proceeds from the sale are being donated to Feeding America, providing at least 10 meals for every dollar raised. This unique diamond was created by The Clorox Company to celebrate National Ranch Day and highlights an innovative method of diamond creation using carbon from buttermilk and milk.

title: Consumers Will Increase Gift Card Purchases in 2022, a Fiserv Study Says
summary: A Fiserv Inc. study indicates that consumers will increase gift card purchases in 2022, primarily due to inflation, supply-chain shortages, and their perception of gift cards as an appropriate alternative to tangible gifts. The study highlights interest in both physical and digital gift cards, with birthdays being the most popular occasion for giving, and Visa Inc. and American Express Co. cards being the most popular brands. Promotions are also expected to drive gift card sales.

title: Opinion: Twitter put Elon Musk on the board so he wouldn’t pull another Tesla
summary: Twitter appointed Elon Musk to its board, preventing a potential hostile takeover. This move avoids a repeat of what happened at Tesla, where Musk became the largest shareholder and eventually took control, despite not being a founder. The board seat agreement also restricts Musk from acquiring more than 15% of Twitter's stock.

title: 11 Etsy sellers explain why they're going on strike
summary: Thousands of Etsy sellers are planning a week-long strike starting April 11th in protest of Etsy's recent fee increase and other policy changes. The strike, organized through Coworker.org, seeks to pressure Etsy to address issues such as rising fees, the "Star Seller" program, mandatory offsite ads, and the presence of mass-produced items on the platform, which sellers argue undermine the handmade ethos of Etsy. Many sellers feel that Etsy has prioritized shareholder profits over the small businesses that built its reputation.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. stock rises Thursday, still underperforms market
summary: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. (LYV) saw a slight increase of 0.03% in its stock price on Thursday, closing at $109.61, despite a strong performance by the overall stock market. The stock remains $18.14 below its 52-week high, and its trading volume was lower than its 50-day average. The company's performance was mixed when compared to competitors like eBay Inc. and World Wrestling Entertainment Inc. Cl A.

title: New eBay Study Finds Gen Z is Driving the Demand for Recommerce
summary: A new eBay study reveals that Gen Z is a primary driver behind the growing demand for recommerce, both as buyers and sellers of secondhand goods. The report highlights that economic opportunity and sustainability are key motivations for participation, with many consumers choosing to sell pre-owned items for extra cash and to support the circular economy. This trend not only creates economic opportunities but also contributes to a more sustainable future by extending the life of products.

title: The Biggest Reveals from Bitcoin 2022
summary: The Bitcoin 2022 conference in Miami highlighted the early stage of Bitcoin adoption, with comparisons drawn to the internet in 1995. Key announcements included Robinhood rolling out crypto wallets and Lightning Network integration, new jurisdictions like Prospera (Honduras) and Madeira adopting Bitcoin as legal tender, and Strike partnering with major point-of-sale systems like Shopify and McDonald's to facilitate Bitcoin payments. The article emphasizes Bitcoin's potential for mass adoption and its disruptive impact on payment processing.

title: eBay, Ericsson and others: Several more companies impose sanctions against Russia
summary: Several more companies, including eBay, Ericsson, Celonis, JetBrains, and ICEYE, have joined sanctions against Russia following the invasion of Ukraine. These companies have halted activities, deliveries, sales, and services in the Russian market, with some also affecting Belarus. Ericsson, for example, is placing employees on paid leave and reserving SEK 0.9 billion for expenses due to the indefinite suspension of its operations.

title: eBay Names Eddie Garcia Chief Product Officer
summary: eBay Inc. has appointed Eddie Garcia as its new Chief Product Officer, effective April 18, 2022. Garcia will lead eBay's Core Product organization, focusing on delivering an improved customer experience as part of the company's tech-led reimagination. He rejoins eBay with over two decades of product leadership experience, having previously worked at Meta, Travelzoo, and Sam's Club.

title: eBay Appoints Eddie Garcia As Next Chief Product Officer
summary: eBay has announced the appointment of Eddie Garcia as its new Chief Product Officer, succeeding Pete Thompson. Garcia, who previously worked at eBay from 2003-2014, returns to the company after serving as CPO at Sam's Club and Head of Commerce at Facebook/Meta. He will lead eBay's Core Product organization, focusing on delivering a streamlined and world-class customer experience as part of the company's tech-led reimagination.

title: eBay Motors Kicks Off Its Inaugural 'New York Auto Parts Show' To Meet the Demands of a Strained Market
summary: eBay Motors launched its first 'New York Auto Parts Show' to address supply chain challenges by demonstrating how car enthusiasts can build, fix, or maintain their dream cars using parts and accessories. The show features "Re-Concept Cars" created in partnership with top auto builders and actress Sydney Sweeney, showcasing how existing vehicles can be given new life. This initiative highlights eBay Motors' role in providing hard-to-find parts for DIY enthusiasts and professional mechanics.

title: Ebay names new chief product officer
summary: Ebay Inc. has announced the appointment of Eddie Garcia as its new chief product officer, effective April 18, 2022. Garcia, who previously held roles at Meta, Travelzoo, and Sam's Club, is returning to Ebay's product organization where he worked from 2003-2014. He will lead Ebay's core product organization and report directly to CEO Jamie Iannone.

title: Former eBay security director to plead guilty to cyberstalking
summary: A former eBay security director, Jim Baugh, is set to plead guilty to leading a cyberstalking campaign against a couple who ran a website critical of eBay. This announcement comes after five other former eBay employees already admitted their roles in the cross-country harassment scheme, which involved sending bizarre and threatening items to the couple. The campaign stemmed from the website's reporting on eBay's then-CEO's compensation package.

title: The Home Depot Names Matt Carey EVP of Customer Experience; Fahim Siddiqui Named EVP and Chief Information Officer
summary: The Home Depot announced two key executive appointments: Matt Carey has been named EVP of Customer Experience, a newly created role focusing on enhancing seamless customer interactions across all platforms. Concurrently, Fahim Siddiqui has been appointed EVP and Chief Information Officer, responsible for the company's technology strategy and infrastructure. These appointments aim to further Home Depot's commitment to customer-centric technology innovation and an interconnected shopping experience.

title: Pari Sawant has been appointed Chief Product Officer at Elavon, Inc.
summary: Pari Sawant has been appointed Chief Product Officer at Elavon, Inc., where he will oversee global product management with over 20 years of experience in consumer, SMB, and enterprise software. Sawant previously led the Document Cloud business and AI capabilities for Adobe.com, and held senior roles at Fiserv, MetricStream, eBay, and Hewlett-Packard. He holds an MBA from UC Berkeley and a Master's in Computer Science from SUNY Binghamton.

title: Home Depot names company vet to CIO role
summary: Home Depot has announced the appointment of Matt Carey as the new executive vice president of customer experience and Fahim Siddiqui as the new executive vice president and chief information officer. Carey will lead strategies to enhance customer experience in stores and digital channels, while Siddiqui will manage digital strategy, infrastructure, and software development. Both promotions highlight Home Depot's focus on internal talent and technology to improve customer interaction and differentiate itself in the home improvement market.

title: Meet eBay’s Chief Sustainability Officer
summary: This article features an interview with Renée Morin, eBay's Chief Sustainability Officer, on Earth Day. She discusses eBay's commitment to sustainability through internal goals, renewable energy, and promoting recommerce. Morin highlights the environmental and economic benefits of secondhand goods and shares her personal passion for sustainability.

title: How an eBay UK Seller Has Turned Recommerce Into a Million-Dollar Business
summary: Carl Walker and his co-founders have built a million-dollar business, Go Thrift, on eBay by selling secondhand clothing. Starting from a small collection of vintage dresses, the company now operates out of a 10,000-square-foot warehouse, focusing on fashion sustainability and global recommerce. Go Thrift's mission is to give new life to pre-loved clothes, keeping them out of landfills and contributing to a healthier planet, while also demonstrating the strong business potential in the growing demand for secondhand goods.

title: Pain relievers recalled over child-safety issue
summary: Telebrands has recalled four of its Hempväna pain relief products due to a child-safety issue. The products, which contain lidocaine, do not have child-resistant packaging as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, posing a poisoning risk to children. Approximately 183,000 units were sold, and while no injuries have been reported, consumers are advised to stop using them and contact Telebrands for disposal information, a refund, or a child-resistant closure.

title: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. ADR underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: On Monday, Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. ADR (BABA) saw its stock slump by 0.75% to $85.84, underperforming the broader market and several key competitors. This movement occurred despite a positive trading session for the NASDAQ Composite Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The company's ADR closed significantly below its 52-week high, while competitors like Amazon.com Inc. and eBay Inc. posted gains.

title: Former eBay security head pleads guilty to harassing Massachusetts couple
summary: James Baugh, eBay's former senior director of safety and security, pleaded guilty to multiple charges, including conspiracy to commit stalking and witness tampering, in connection with a harassment campaign against a Massachusetts couple. The campaign involved sending disturbing deliveries, online threats, and attempting to install a GPS tracking device on their car. Several other former eBay employees have also pleaded guilty in the case, which stemmed from the couple publishing a newsletter critical of the company.

title: eBay's Authenticity Guarantee Now Includes Graded Trading Cards Through Partnership with PSA
summary: eBay has expanded its 'Authenticity Guarantee' service to include graded trading cards, partnering with PSA (Professional Sports Authenticator) for verification. Initially, all graded cards sold for $2,000+ in the U.S. will be authenticated by PSA, with plans to extend this service to cards sold for $250+. This initiative aims to bolster buyer confidence and ensure the integrity of the rapidly growing trading card market.

title: eBay Launches 2022 'Up & Running Grants' to Support Small Business Success
summary: eBay has launched its third annual Up & Running Grants program, committing $10,000 grants to 50 small business sellers in the U.S. This initiative aims to help entrepreneurs scale, grow, and thrive online by providing funds for business needs like store improvements, equipment, and hiring. Recipients will also gain access to eBay Academy's online learning content and direct coaching to support their success.

title: Alberta used numbered company in legal battle to save Keystone XL pipeline
summary: The Alberta government, through its Alberta Petroleum Marketing Commission, established several numbered companies in Delaware and Alberta to invest in the ill-fated Keystone XL pipeline project with TC Energy. This arrangement allowed the province to invest up to $7.5 billion in public money and now pursue a $1.3 billion damages claim against the U.S. under NAFTA's legacy rules, despite the project's cancellation by President Biden. The use of obscure Delaware companies highlights efforts to preserve the investment and legal standing, even as the auditor general confirms significant losses.

title: The Chernin Group-Led Consortium to Acquire $263 Million in Funko, a Leading Pop Culture Lifestyle Brand
summary: A consortium led by The Chernin Group (TCG) will acquire a $263 million strategic investment in Funko, a pop culture lifestyle brand, taking an 80% stake from ACON Investments. This all-secondary transaction values Funko shares at $21.00 each, with TCG and its consortium owning 25% of the company post-acquisition. The investment includes commercial agreements with eBay Inc. and strategic advising for Funko's growth in content, commerce, and new product categories.

title: 20 trustworthy online money-making sites in Kenya in 2026
summary: This article lists 20 trustworthy online platforms where Kenyans can earn money, detailing various opportunities such as freelancing, affiliate marketing, online surveys, and AI training. It provides guidance for beginners on how to get started, emphasizing that many platforms require minimal skills and can lead to significant income with consistent effort. The article also touches on how to earn money specifically via M-Pesa and clarifies which sites are legitimate for making money online in Kenya.

title: Our 2021 Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Report
summary: eBay has released its sixth annual Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DE&I) Report, highlighting a sharpened focus on growing underrepresented employee groups, fostering community, and enhancing accountability for sustainable change. The report emphasizes the integration of grassroots efforts with leadership support, tools, education, and process improvements. Notably, oversight of DE&I has been expanded to the Compensation and Human Capital Committee on eBay’s Board, with executive officer compensation now tied to DE&I goals, marking a significant step toward senior-level accountability.

title: eBay and FedEx Extend Alliance in Canada with Shipping Labels Platform Integration
summary: eBay and FedEx have extended their alliance in Canada, integrating FedEx shipping services directly into eBay Labels. This collaboration offers Canadian e-commerce sellers seamless and cost-effective shipping solutions, including access to domestic and international FedEx services at preferential rates. The move aims to simplify the shipping process for eBay sellers, reflecting the significant growth in e-commerce driven by pre-pandemic projections now hitting earlier than expected.

title: eBay partners with FedEx to ease Canadian shipping
summary: eBay has partnered with FedEx to integrate FedEx shipping services directly into its Canadian online marketplace, eBay Labels. This collaboration aims to provide Canadian sellers with seamless access to domestic and international shipping tools and services at preferential rates, simplifying the shipping process. The partnership also extends FedEx's broader efforts to streamline e-commerce fulfillment for retailers, building on its existing collaboration with Microsoft for "logistics as a service."

title: eBay Releases Methodology for Calculating Environmental and Financial Benefits of Recommerce and 2021 Impact Report
summary: eBay has released its methodology for calculating the environmental and financial benefits of recommerce, providing transparency on how avoided carbon emissions and waste impacts are determined. The company also published its 2021 Impact Report, detailing progress toward its 2025 and 2030 sustainability goals, including generating $4 billion in positive economic impact and avoiding significant carbon emissions and waste in 2021. This initiative aims to empower consumers to make informed choices when purchasing pre-owned items and encourages other companies to track their environmental impact.

title: eBay Workforce Diversity & Corporate Culture
summary: eBay's latest Diversity Report indicates improvements in gender diversity within leadership and tech roles, but persistent underrepresentation for Black and Latinx employees, particularly in technical and executive positions. In response, eBay has launched a new "Culture & Workforce" page to attract job seekers, highlighting perks and diversity initiatives. The article also touches upon ongoing scrutiny of eBay's corporate culture following a 2019 cyberstalking scandal, suggesting a need for significant cultural rehabilitation efforts.

title: JD.com Opens First Overseas Service Centers in the US
summary: JD.com has opened two new overseas service centers in Los Angeles and New Jersey, marking its first such venture in the overseas market. These centers will offer comprehensive aftersales and maintenance services for electronics and appliances, supported by JD's air cargo lines and self-operated warehouses in the US. The initiative aims to provide end-to-end supply chain logistics services and reduce repair times for brands and cross-border merchants.

title: Snapchat Reveals Integration With eBay
summary: Snapchat has integrated with eBay, allowing users to share eBay listings directly through the Snapchat camera on both Android and iOS devices. This feature enables Snapchatters to share items they are interested in or selling with friends, and also helps eBay sellers promote their listings across platforms. The integration aims to connect eBay's large buyer base with Snapchat's social sharing capabilities.

title: eBay opens a sneaker store in Los Angeles
summary: eBay is opening a brick-and-mortar sneaker store called "Wear ‘Em Out" on Fairfax Avenue in Los Angeles during Memorial Day weekend. The store will offer exclusive stock of new styles and collectibles. Shoppers who choose to wear their new sneakers out of the store will receive an additional discount, encouraging them to actually wear their purchases rather than keeping them "on ice."

title: eBay Launches First Collection of NFTs in Partnership with Green Web3 Company OneOf
summary: eBay has launched its first collection of NFTs in partnership with OneOf, a green Web3 company. The "Genesis" NFT Collection features 3D and animated interpretations of Sports Illustrated covers of iconic athletes, starting with Wayne Gretzky. This collaboration aims to make NFTs more accessible to a new generation of collectors, leveraging eBay's global reach and OneOf's eco-conscious technology.

title: EBay to open sneaker pop-up in LA
summary: EBay is launching a "Wear 'Em Out Store" pop-up in Los Angeles, dedicated to sneakers, which offers lower prices to customers who wear their purchases out of the store. This initiative, supported by rapper Offset, aims to encourage sneakerheads to wear their collections rather than store them. The pop-up, running Memorial Day weekend, is part of eBay's broader strategy to boost its sneaker category, following investments in authenticity verification and the acquisition of Sneaker Con's authentication arm.

title: PayPal Lays Off Employees As Growth Stagnates After Pandemic Recovery, Supply Chain Disruptions
summary: PayPal has begun laying off employees in risk management and operations roles across several U.S. cities. These job cuts are part of a broader restructuring effort aimed at saving approximately $260 million annually in employee-related costs. The company's growth has stagnated as platform spending slowed significantly in the first quarter, attributed to supply chain disruptions and a return to in-store shopping post-pandemic.

title: eBay launches a new ‘Vault’ facility for storing valuable trading cards
summary: eBay has launched a new 31,000-square-foot "Vault" facility in Delaware for securely storing valuable trading cards. This service allows cards valued over $750, authenticated by PSA, to be stored, insured, and quickly traded digitally without physical shipping. The initiative aims to reduce risks associated with shipping and streamline transactions for collectors like DJ Skee.

title: The IRS Is Coming for Your Venmo Income
summary: Small-business owners and individuals using platforms like Venmo, PayPal, eBay, and Airbnb will face increased IRS scrutiny due to a new rule. The IRS now requires these services to report gross payments exceeding $600 directly, a significant change from the previous $20,000 threshold. This means more individuals will receive a 1099-K form and need to track their income and sales to ensure compliance, even for casual selling.

title: Women of Influence 2022: Lakshimi Duraivenkatesh of eBay Inc.
summary: Lakshimi Duraivenkatesh, Vice President of Buyer Experience Engineering at eBay, has been honored as a 2022 Woman of Influence by the Silicon Valley Business Journal. This recognition highlights her significant role and contributions within eBay. The article also references other groups of honorees for the 2022 Women of Influence award.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. (LYV) stock fell 1.03% on Thursday, underperforming the broader market but still showing mixed results against competitors. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, despite some competitors also seeing losses or small gains. Trading volume was lower than its 50-day average.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. stock (LYV) fell 1.71% on Friday, closing at $92.31, amid a broader market downturn with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also experiencing significant losses. Despite its daily decline, the stock outperformed some competitors, such as eBay Inc. and World Wrestling Entertainment Inc. The company's stock is currently $35.44 below its 52-week high.

title: eBay Launches Its Vault for Trading Cards
summary: eBay has launched its new vault service for trading cards, a 31,000 square foot, 24/7 secured facility designed for collectors. The vault allows for instant sales and ownership transfers of graded trading cards valued at $750 or more, streamlining transactions and enhancing security through eBay's Authenticity Guarantee. By 2023, the service plans to expand to other collectibles, with an aim to store up to $3 billion in assets within a few years.

title: Artificial Intelligence at eBay – Two Current Use-Cases
summary: eBay, a global e-commerce leader with 147 million buyers worldwide, leverages AI in two key areas: enhanced product classification and image processing. By using text and image similarity models, eBay aims to improve the accuracy and scalability of product categorization, leading to better search results and higher customer satisfaction. Additionally, the company employs computer vision AI, specifically its "Image-Clean Up" feature, to help sellers remove distracting backgrounds from item photos, making products more appealing and building buyer trust.

title: eBay Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock dropped 0.82% to $42.58 on Tuesday, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines. This marked the fourth consecutive day of losses for eBay, with the stock closing significantly below its 52-week high. Competitors showed mixed performance, with Amazon.com Inc. falling, while Walmart Inc. and Costco Wholesale Corp. rose.

title: The Surprising Case for Stronger E-commerce Growth
summary: Despite a recent slump in e-commerce stocks and slowing economic activity, Morgan Stanley forecasts continued strong growth for e-commerce, projecting an increase from $3.3 trillion today to $5.4 trillion in 2026. This growth is driven by a permanent behavioral shift to online shopping, increasing penetration in both developed and emerging markets, and technological improvements in logistics and digital payments.

title: Everything You Wanted to Know About Bear Markets
summary: This article discusses the characteristics of bear markets, noting that while bull markets are similar, bear markets each have unique causes. Author Frank Holmes explains that the current bear market is due to pandemic-related liquidity, rampant inflation, and aggressive central bank actions, including a recent significant interest rate hike by the Federal Reserve. He advises investors to stay diversified, consider adding gold to their portfolios, and maintain a positive outlook given that bear markets are typically short-lived and can present buying opportunities.

title: eBay Launches Live Shopping for Collectibles
summary: eBay has launched eBay Live, a new dedicated live shopping platform for collectibles that combines entertainment with instant purchasing. The platform's inaugural event will feature rare trading cards from top seller Bleecker Trading, hosted by DJ Skee. This initiative expands eBay's offerings for collectors, following recent advancements like its trading card vault and Authenticity Guarantee service.

title: The Big Interview: Expedia Group CTO Rathi Murthy
summary: Expedia Group CTO Rathi Murthy discusses the company's "Expedia Open World" vision, a new API-centric, modular travel platform designed to allow more flexible integration for partners. She details the challenges of this large-scale transformation while the company is actively operating, emphasizing a "people, process, technology" approach and an iterative, accountability-driven methodology. The article highlights Expedia's move to AWS and their focus on hiring diverse talent.

title: GLIDE, eBay and Warren Buffett Celebrate the Grand Finale Power of One Charity Auction Lunch With Record-Breaking Winning Bid
summary: The 21st annual Power of One Charity Auction Lunch with Warren Buffett, hosted by GLIDE and eBay, concluded with a record-breaking winning bid of $19,000,100. This grand finale auction has cumulatively raised over $53 million for GLIDE's programs which combat poverty, hunger, and homelessness. The anonymous winner will enjoy a private lunch with Warren Buffett, continuing a tradition that has significantly supported GLIDE's mission.

title: eBay to Close GittiGidiyor Operations
summary: eBay Inc. announced its decision to close its Turkish marketplace business, GittiGidiyor, due to competitive market dynamics. Sellers will no longer be able to list new items as of today, and buyers cannot make new purchases after July 18. The closure is not expected to materially impact eBay's Q2 or full-year financial results.

title: eBay to close Turkish platform GittiGidiyor over market dynamics | Daily Sabah
summary: E-commerce giant eBay Inc. is closing its Turkish marketplace, GittiGidiyor, a decision influenced by competitive market dynamics in Turkey. The platform, fully owned by eBay since 2016, will stop new listings immediately and close for shopping by July 18. Despite contributing 4 million active buyers in Q1 2022, eBay expects the closure to have minimal impact on its financial results.

title: EBay acquires NFT marketplace KnownOrigin
summary: EBay has acquired KnownOrigin, a significant non-fungible token (NFT) marketplace, to enhance its digital collectibles strategy and Web3 capabilities. This acquisition comes about a year after eBay allowed NFT trading on its own platform, marking a deeper dive into cryptographic assets. KnownOrigin's founders expressed enthusiasm for joining eBay's "tech-led reimagination" aimed at modernizing its marketplace and expanding into digital collectibles.

title: eBay Acquires Leading NFT Marketplace, KnownOrigin
summary: eBay Inc. has acquired KnownOrigin, a leading non-fungible token (NFT) marketplace founded in 2018. This acquisition aims to enhance eBay's position in digital collectibles and expand its community of sellers and buyers to include NFT creators and collectors. The deal closed on June 21, 2022, and reflects eBay's strategy to reimagine its tech-led platform by integrating new digital collecting opportunities.

title: Sports Illustrated Partners With Muhammad Ali Enterprises for New NFT Drop With OneOf and eBay
summary: Sports Illustrated (SI) and OneOf have announced a new NFT collection honoring Muhammad Ali, which will be available exclusively on eBay for a limited time. The collection will feature 3D-animated NFTs of Ali, paying homage to his 40th and final Sports Illustrated cover. This initiative builds on OneOf's partnership with eBay, aiming to connect fans with iconic athletes and brands through sustainable, energy-efficient NFTs.

title: The Apple-1 Computer, a Vintage Tech Grail That Ignited the World of Personal Computing
summary: The article discusses the Apple-1 computer, a rare vintage tech item that played a pivotal role in the inception of personal computing. Only about 82 of the original 200 units are believed to still exist, with one recently selling for approximately $340,000 on eBay. The device, which required users to provide a power supply, keyboard, storage, and display, was groundbreaking for offering a complete circuit board during a time when most computers were sold as DIY kits.

title: Should You Sell Your Construction Equipment Online?
summary: The article discusses the growing trend of selling construction equipment online, accelerated by the pandemic's impact on e-commerce. It highlights how platforms like eBay provide a global reach and a secure marketplace, leading to high demand and increased profit for sellers. Contractors are encouraged to leverage these online tools due to existing supply chain shortages and a shift towards online purchasing behaviors.

title: What are the biggest spin-off companies of all time?
summary: This article explores some of the largest corporate spin-offs in history, detailing the reasons behind these separations and their financial outcomes. It highlights notable examples such as eBay and PayPal, the breakup of the Bell System, Altria and Phillip Morris, and the dissolution of DowDuPont, among others. The article discusses the initial valuations, subsequent stock performance, and revenues of both the parent companies and their spun-off entities.

title: Florida man charged with selling fake Cisco equipment in $1 billion scheme
summary: A Florida man, Onur Aksoy, has been charged with importing and reselling counterfeit Cisco networking equipment worth over $1 billion if authentic. Aksoy allegedly operated a scheme from 2013 to 2022, netting $100 million in revenue by selling fake devices through Amazon.com, eBay, and other platforms after acquiring them from suppliers in China and Hong Kong. Cisco Systems Inc. sent multiple cease-and-desist letters, and both Amazon and eBay blocked numerous storefronts related to the counterfeiting.

title: eBay Ventures First Investment is Norwegian-Based Recommerce App Tise
summary: eBay Ventures, the new venture capital arm of eBay, has made its first investment in Tise, a Norwegian reuse app focused on second-hand fashion, interior, and furniture. Tise raised NOK 200 million (nearly $20M) from eBay Ventures, Nysnø, and Bring Ventures, aiming to expand its reach in European markets. This cautious first investment for eBay Ventures highlights Tise's strong performance metrics and a shared passion for sustainable re-commerce.

title: Ebay’s Love Island collaboration causes surge in pre-loved popularity
summary: Ebay's partnership with the reality show Love Island has led to a significant increase in searches for "pre-loved fashion" on both Ebay and Google. The collaboration aims to promote sustainable fashion choices among viewers, with contestants wearing second-hand items. Research indicates a growing preference for pre-loved and sustainable brands, particularly among Millennials, although younger generations are still divided between pre-loved and fast fashion.

title: Authorities announce plans for site of soon-to-be former Utah prison
summary: Authorities have revealed plans for "The Point," the 600-acre site of Utah's former correctional facility in Draper. This development aims to create Utah's Innovation Community, situated within the rapidly growing tech corridor. The project includes plans for 3,000 housing units in phase one, with 12.5% designated as affordable housing, addressing concerns about housing costs and water resources in the region.

title: Letter: eBay Charity Program - Net vs Gross Proceeds
summary: A seller participating in eBay's Charity Program highlights a significant discrepancy in terminology when donating 100% of a sale. The listing incorrectly states "net proceeds" will benefit charity, whereas the seller is actually donating "gross proceeds" as all fees must still be covered out of pocket. The author recounts frustrating attempts to get clarification and resolution from multiple eBay representatives, who provided inconsistent and incorrect explanations, ultimately leading to a complaint with the Better Business Bureau.

title: eBay Launches Authentication for Fine Jewelry
summary: eBay has expanded its "Authenticity Guarantee" service to include fine jewelry, in collaboration with the Gemological Institute of America (GIA). This service will authenticate eligible new and pre-owned fine jewelry sold for $500 or more, with GIA experts performing multi-point inspections. The new initiative aims to enhance trust for both buyers and sellers in eBay's growing luxury category, building on the success of authentication programs for sneakers, watches, handbags, and trading cards.

title: Receiving money via apps? Selling items online? It may affect your taxes
summary: New tax laws, specifically the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), have changed the reporting requirements for online sales. For the 2025 calendar year (2026 filing season), a 1099-K form will be issued if transactions exceed $20,000 and there are over 200 transactions. This article from H&R Block explains how to report online sales, whether for personal items, hobbies, or businesses, and provides guidance on tax forms like 1099-K, Schedule D, and Form 8949.

title: Crater graduate launches sneaker side-hustle into growing entrepreneurship
summary: Colton Vranes, a Crater graduate, successfully transformed a COVID-19 side-hustle of reselling sneakers into a profitable business named Reselling Secrets, now bringing in $100,000 monthly. The venture began with reselling his and his brother's shoes, quickly expanded with a Discord server, and has since opened a brick-and-mortar store in Pennsylvania. Vranes plans to continue growing his business while attending Brigham Young University and anticipates being named Entrepreneur of the Year.

title: Earnings: eBay Q2 revenue down 9%; profit beats estimates
summary: E-commerce firm eBay Inc. (NASDAQ: EBAY) reported unchanged adjusted earnings for the second quarter of 2022 at $0.99 per share, beating analyst estimates, despite a 9% decrease in revenues to $2.42 billion. The company experienced a net loss on an unadjusted basis for the quarter. eBay's stock saw gains in Wednesday's trading following the announcement.

title: eBay Foundation’s Global Give Program Grants $3M Toward Inclusive Entrepreneurship
summary: The eBay Foundation announced its annual Global Give program is providing $3 million in grants to 30 nonprofit organizations worldwide. This year's grants focus on supporting inclusive entrepreneurship for historically excluded groups, reflecting a shift towards trust-based philanthropy. The foundation aims to foster deeper relationships with grantees and help remove barriers to economic opportunity.

title: The African “Silicon Valley” Underway
summary: South Africa, particularly Cape Town and the Western Cape, is emerging as a strong contender for "the next Silicon Valley" due to its robust tech ecosystem, high foreign direct investment, and a significant number of tech startups and venture capital firms. The region boasts a high concentration of developer talent, world-class universities, and an environment conducive to doing business, mirroring key factors that contributed to the success and sustained innovation of the original Silicon Valley. These attributes, combined with a focus on cultural diversity and strong educational institutions, position South Africa as an innovation hub on the African continent.

title: The North Face is leading the refurbished product proposition
summary: The North Face is expanding its Renewed resale program to boost sales and enhance its circularity efforts, building on its 21% revenue growth since 2020. The company has partnered with Tersus for waterless cleaning and Archive for an e-commerce platform that details product lifecycles. This initiative aims to attract price-sensitive customers, fashion enthusiasts interested in vintage items, and those committed to circular business models, with plans for a peer-to-peer operation and a new capsule collection.

title: Love Island style icon, Tasha Ghouri, signs game-changing fashion deal with eBay UK
summary: eBay UK has signed Love Island star Tasha Ghouri as its first-ever 'Pre-Loved Ambassador,' marking the first non-fast fashion brand partnership to emerge from the reality TV series. This collaboration aims to promote secondhand fashion and change consumer shopping habits, aligning with eBay's mission to highlight sustainable clothing choices. Ghouri, recognized for her distinctive style on the show, will work with eBay to advocate for responsible and conscious shopping that benefits both buyers and the environment.

title: eBay Launches First Collection of Exclusive Funko Products
summary: eBay announced a new collaboration with Funko to launch a series of exclusive Funko products, starting with Vinyl GOLD™. This partnership aims to provide collectors with access to rare and coveted Funko items, leveraging eBay's global reach and established collectibles marketplace. The first exclusive product is available now, with additional drops planned for later in the year.

title: Worksport Launches All-New E-Commerce Online Platform
summary: Worksport Ltd. has launched a new e-Commerce platform to sell its proprietary truck bed tonneau covers, including the SC3, SC3 Pro, and SC4. The platform, shopworksport.com, will later feature its hard cover TC3 and flagship products SOLIS (solar-integrated tonneau cover) and COR (portable energy storage system). This initiative aims to streamline production and sales, with products also available on Walmart, Amazon, and eBay.

title: How eBay Is Helping Southeast Asia’s Small Businesses Succeed
summary: eBay's new report, the Southeast Asia Small Online Business Trade Report, highlights how small businesses in six Southeast Asian countries are thriving through e-commerce, exporting to an average of 25 different countries. This global reach, often to markets inaccessible through traditional channels, has allowed 99% of eBay-enabled businesses in the region to export their goods, compared to only 11% of traditional businesses. The report also notes that 70% of these successful small businesses were new to eBay, joining after 2016 and exceeding the $10,000 annual sales mark in 2020.

title: EBay to acquire collectible tech platform TCGplayer
summary: eBay announced its agreement to acquire TCGplayer, a technology platform for the collectibles industry, for approximately $295 million, expected to close in Q1 2023. TCGplayer, specializing in trading card games, will operate independently, enhancing eBay's focus on the growing collectibles market and providing capabilities like order fulfillment and cart optimization. This acquisition builds on eBay's previous investments in the trading card category, including expanding Authenticity Guarantee and launching the eBay Vault.

title: EBay set to acquire online trading card marketplace TCGplayer
summary: E-commerce giant eBay announced its plan to acquire TCGplayer, an online marketplace for trading cards, for up to $295 million. This acquisition, expected to close in Q1 2023, aims to enhance eBay's offerings in the rapidly growing trading card sector. TCGplayer will operate autonomously while benefiting from eBay's resources and experience in connecting hobbyists and communities.

title: eBay Acquires the myFitment Group of Companies to Enhance Part and Accessories Listing Experience
summary: eBay has acquired myFitment, a company specializing in tools and support for online automotive and powersports parts and accessories sellers. This acquisition aims to enhance eBay's parts and accessories category by improving fitment data optimization, which will help customers find the correct parts, reduce returns, and increase buyer satisfaction. The move is part of eBay's tech-led reimagination strategy to provide innovative tools and simplify the listing and buying process for vehicle parts.

title: Problems With eBay Motors Parts & Accessories Fitment
summary: eBay recently acquired myFitment to improve the listing experience for parts and accessories, a category where it has long struggled with fitment and compatibility data. Despite a previous acquisition of WHI Solutions in 2012 for similar purposes, sellers continue to report persistent bugs and execution problems with eBay's fitment chart functionality. The article highlights an example where slight discrepancies in data input prevent the compatibility tool from accurately identifying parts, raising questions about whether the myFitment acquisition will genuinely resolve these core issues.

title: eBay Inc. Announces Changes to its Board of Directors
summary: eBay Inc. announced the appointment of Aparna Chennapragada to its Board of Directors, effective immediately, bringing significant technology and product expertise. Concurrently, Katie Mitic will step down from the board on September 15, 2022, after serving since 2011. The board will now consist of nine directors, eight of whom are independent, maintaining a diverse mix of skills and backgrounds.

title: Crypto ATM Operator Bitcoin Depot to List on Nasdaq in $885M SPAC Deal
summary: Bitcoin Depot, the largest operator of crypto ATMs globally, plans to go public on Nasdaq through a merger with SPAC GSR II Meteora, valuing the company at an estimated $885 million. The deal, expected to close by Q1 2023, will allow users to buy crypto with cash or debit cards without exchanges. However, crypto ATMs have also been linked to scams, prompting regulatory warnings, such as the UK's FCA deeming them illegal.

title: KKR, Anchor Equity tipped to exit from TMON in 7 yrs
summary: KKR and Anchor Equity Partners are reportedly exiting their majority stake in South Korean e-commerce platform TMON Inc. after seven years. The private equity firms are expected to swap TMON shares for those in Singapore-based Qoo10, which is currently preparing for a Nasdaq listing. This deal would allow Qoo10 to gain a foothold in South Korea's competitive e-commerce market.

title: Only on eBay: These Rare Memorabilia Show Serena Williams’s Dominance in Tennis
summary: eBay is selling rare U.S. Open tennis nets authenticated by MeiGray Group, including those used in Serena Williams' 2013 and 2014 singles championship wins. MeiGray Group, founded by Barry Meisel and Bob Gray, was the official authenticator for the USTA for four years, ensuring the legitimacy of sports memorabilia. This sale offers tennis enthusiasts a unique piece of history from Serena Williams' dominant career.

title: 10. Peter Thiel
summary: Peter Thiel, co-founder of PayPal and early investor in Facebook, has a net worth of $7 billion, marking a 19% increase from the previous year. He co-founded Palantir Technologies Inc. and holds a significant stake in the data-analytics company. Thiel is also known for managing a substantial portion of his portfolio through a tax-exempt Roth IRA and is a major conservative political donor.

title: eBay Brings Pimp My Ride Back for a New Six-Part Series
summary: MTV U.K. and eBay have partnered to revive the popular automotive makeover show "Pimp My Ride" for a new six-part YouTube series, hosted by British rapper Lady Leshurr. The series focuses on transforming vehicles using cost-effective and environmentally friendly car parts sourced in part from eBay, promoting upcycling and DIY car modifications. The first episode premiered on August 19th on MTV U.K.'s YouTube channel.

title: eBay Chief Technology Officer Mazen Rawashdeh Talks Culture, Strategy and Taking Time Off
summary: eBay CTO Mazen Rawashdeh recently appeared on the Modern CTO podcast, discussing his return from sabbatical and its importance for productivity. He highlighted eBay's culture, the vital role of AI and machine learning, and how to build strong corporate values where employees "do the right thing when you're not watching." Rawashdeh also touched on eBay's strategic position in the tech space and its approach to competition.

title: At eBay, Lurid Crimes and the Search for Punishment (Published 2022)
summary: This article details a cyberstalking campaign orchestrated by eBay employees against a couple, Ina and David Steiner, who ran an e-commerce publication. The harassment, which included sending disturbing items and making false online posts, began after former eBay CEO Devin Wenig expressed anger over a story. The piece explores the questions of responsibility and punishment for both the individuals involved and the corporation.

title: Janet Bannister leaving Real Ventures as venture capital firm faces uncertain future
summary: Janet Bannister, a prominent figure in Canadian venture capital, is departing Real Ventures amid the firm's struggles to raise a new fund. Her exit raises questions about the future of Real Ventures, a firm that played a significant role in reviving Canada's tech sector, as it now lacks fresh capital for new investments. Despite this, Real's remaining founding partner, John Stokes, asserts the firm is not winding down and will focus on managing its existing investments.

title: Fig. 1 Patents law firm grows
summary: Fig. 1 Patents PLLC, a law firm established in 2020 specializing in patent preparation, is experiencing rapid growth with a projected 20% annual revenue increase. The firm, located in downtown Spokane, anticipates doubling its revenue in the next few years and plans a significant hiring spree to keep pace with demand from major tech clients like eBay, Adobe, and Motorola Mobility. Additionally, Fig. 1 Patents has partnered with Gonzaga University School of Law and eBay to create a scholarship and internship program aimed at encouraging diversity within intellectual property law, an industry that requires both STEM and legal degrees.

title: 'The priorities were a little baffling': Evite CEO David Yeom on transforming the platform's business model
summary: Evite CEO David Yeom discusses the transformation of the digital invitation platform's business model. Under his leadership, Evite shifted from an ad-based revenue stream to commerce, focusing on premium invitations and gifting, and redesigned its brand with in-house talent. The changes led to profitability in 2021 and plans for international expansion.

title: eBay Motors to Sponsor Sarah and Bridget Burgess in Historic Mother-Daughter ARCA Race
summary: eBay Motors will be sponsoring Sarah and Bridget Burgess in the ARCA Menards West Series race on October 14, 2022, marking the first time a mother and daughter have raced against each other in NASCAR and ARCA history. Sarah Burgess, who also works for eBay Motors, will be racing the number 97 eBay Motors Chevrolet SS, while her daughter Bridget will also compete. The event will take place at The Bullring in Las Vegas, NV, and will be live-streamed on FloRacing.

title: eBay User Agreement Changes 9-28-22
summary: eBay has updated its User Agreement, effective October 28, 2022, as part of its Fall 2022 Seller Update. Key changes include terms for Indian users, clarification on seller payment authorization, details on the eBay International Shipping Program, and increased responsibility for user fraud losses for both buyers and sellers. These updates aim to address marketplace facilitator laws and protect eBay from fraud-related financial losses.

title: Inside the bizarre harassment scheme that’s sending former EBay execs to prison
summary: Two former eBay executives, James Baugh and David Harville, were sentenced to prison for leading a harassment campaign against the creators of an online newsletter. The campaign involved sending disturbing deliveries like live spiders and a funeral wreath, orchestrated in retaliation for critical coverage of eBay. The victims expressed fear that such schemes could set a precedent for companies targeting journalists.

title: Ex-eBay execs heading to prison for harassing couple behind newsletter
summary: Two former eBay Inc. security executives, Jim Baugh and David Harville, were sentenced to prison for leading a harassment campaign against a Massachusetts couple who published an online newsletter critical of eBay. The campaign included sending disturbing items to the couple's home and cyberstalking. Their actions were reportedly fueled by a "toxic culture" at eBay and anger from the company's then-CEO regarding the newsletter.

title: Live spiders and cockroaches: Ex-eBay executives get prison time in harassment plot
summary: Former eBay Inc. executives, James Baugh and David Harville, have received prison sentences for orchestrating a harassment campaign against the creators of an online newsletter, David and Ina Steiner. The scheme involved sending disturbing items like live spiders, cockroaches, and a funeral wreath to their home. Both convicted executives apologized for their actions, which stemmed from negative coverage of eBay in the couple's newsletter and an executive's message to "take her down."

title: Former eBay executives sentenced for cyberstalking married couple
summary: Two former eBay executives, Jim Baugh and David Harville, were sentenced in connection with a 2019 cyberstalking case targeting a Massachusetts couple, David and Ina Steiner. The executives orchestrated a campaign of harassment, sending disturbing items and attempting to install a GPS tracker, because the couple's blog criticized eBay. Baugh received four years and nine months in prison, while Harville was sentenced to two years, as the victims pursue a civil lawsuit against eBay and its former CEO.

title: GALLERY: Meta data center expansion announced in Eagle Mountain
summary: Meta, Facebook's parent company, announced an expansion of its data center in Eagle Mountain, Utah, with five buildings already constructed. The third phase of the expansion introduces a new campus, bringing Meta's total investment to $1.5 billion. This expanded capacity will support the Metaverse and is part of a growing trend of data centers in Utah, which also includes facilities by eBay, Twitter, and Oracle.

title: 1962 Amazing Spider-Man Comic Makes eBay History
summary: A 1962 edition of Amazing Fantasy #15, featuring Spider-Man's first appearance, recently sold for $65,100 on eBay, making it the third top-selling comic book on the platform. The sale coincides with Spider-Man's 60th anniversary and highlights the increasing value of rare comic books as long-term investments. The comic was sold by Ted Straka, whose friend had purchased and graded the item years prior, seeing its potential value.

title: Ex-eBay employees sentenced for harassing couple behind newsletter
summary: Two former eBay security employees, Stephanie Popp and Stephanie Stockwell, have been sentenced for their involvement in a cyberstalking campaign against a Massachusetts couple who published an online newsletter critical of eBay. Popp received a year in prison, while Stockwell was given a year of home confinement. The harassment campaign in 2019 included sending disturbing items like cockroaches, fly larvae, and a bloody pig mask to the couple's home.

title: Judge sentences ex-eBay employees for roles in harassment scheme
summary: Two former eBay employees were sentenced for their roles in a harassment campaign against a newsletter's creators, which involved sending disturbing items like live spiders and a bloody pig mask. Stephanie Popp, a senior manager, received prison time, while Stephanie Stockwell, a manager, was given probation. This scheme was orchestrated by former director James Baugh, who has already been sentenced.

title: Former eBay execs. Stephanie Popp, Stephanie Stockwell sentenced in cyberstalking case
summary: Former eBay employees Stephanie Popp and Stephanie Stockwell were sentenced in Boston federal court for cyberstalking a Massachusetts couple. Popp received a year and a day in prison, while Stockwell was given two years of probation with one year of home confinement. They were part of a team that harassed the couple, who published a newsletter critical of eBay, by sending disturbing deliveries and making threats.

title: Better Fintech Stock: PayPal or Upstart
summary: This article compares PayPal (PYPL) and Upstart (UPST), two fintech stocks that have underperformed due to rising interest rates and other macro headwinds. It highlights PayPal's struggles with slowing growth, broken promises, and internal issues, while Upstart faces challenges as its AI-driven lending model is impacted by increasing interest rates. Despite both facing significant hurdles, the author concludes that PayPal is the better buy due to its current profitability, cheaper valuation, and large user base, assuming its management improves its execution.

title: Halloween costume ideas: eBay is removing Jeffrey Dahmer costumes after newly sparked interest in notorious serial killer
summary: eBay is removing listings for Jeffrey Dahmer costumes due to its "Violence and Violent Criminal Policy," which prohibits items promoting or glorifying violence or violent acts. This action comes after a surge of interest in the serial killer following the release of Netflix's "Monster: The Jeffrey Dahmer Story." Despite the ban, similar items are still found on other platforms like Etsy.

title: Post-college, BerDale Robins tackles clothing line, coaching, prepping for pro push
summary: Former Nevada defensive back BerDale Robins is venturing into entrepreneurship with his clothing line, Take Money Clothing, which he started in 2017. He is also pursuing his dream of playing professional football while recovering from an injury and engaging in coaching and charitable activities. Robins discussed his business journey, drawing inspiration from figures like Steve Jobs and Nipsey Hussle, and his plans for expanding his brand.

title: eBay Acquires TCGplayer
summary: eBay Inc. announced it has completed its acquisition of TCGplayer for approximately $295 million. This acquisition aims to enhance the overall collecting experience for enthusiasts by combining eBay's expertise in trading cards with TCGplayer's e-commerce platform and omnichannel capabilities. TCGplayer will continue to operate autonomously, leveraging eBay's resources to further connect hobbyists and sellers with collectors worldwide.

title: Fostering a More Accessible, Tech-Savvy Community of Sellers
summary: This article highlights Greg Perry, an eBay seller with physical disabilities who has been selling on the platform since 1999. He shares his story of leveraging his writing skills and adapting to technological changes to build a successful eBay business, emphasizing the platform's community feel and flexibility. Greg now leads a Facebook group to help other sellers, advocating for increased accessibility in e-commerce and commending eBay's efforts in this area.

title: Pitney Bowes, eBay extend cross-border shipping partnership
summary: Pitney Bowes and eBay have extended their cross-border shipping partnership, with Pitney Bowes continuing to provide technology, compliance, and delivery services for eBay's new International Shipping service. This renewal builds on a decade-long collaboration, aiming to simplify international selling for eBay customers in the U.S. and U.K. The partnership highlights Pitney Bowes' ongoing investments in e-commerce infrastructure, including new hubs, enhanced tracking, and the adoption of AI-powered robotics and autonomous trucks to improve parcel delivery efficiency.

title: eBay’s 2022 Small Business Report: Creating Economic Opportunity For All
summary: eBay's 2022 Small Business Report highlights the platform's crucial role in empowering small businesses, with 71% of sellers heavily relying on eBay and many stating their businesses wouldn't exist without it. The report emphasizes accessibility for all entrepreneurs, especially women and individuals with disabilities, and details how eBay supports sellers through various initiatives, global reach, and the promotion of recommerce. It also notes that many sellers are 'accidental entrepreneurs' who turn their passions into profitable businesses on the platform.

title: eBay Has A New Chief Marketing Officer - Adrian Fung
summary: Adrian Fung has been promoted to eBay's Global Chief Marketing Officer, as announced by Chief Growth Officer Julie Loeger. Fung, who joined eBay in July 2021, previously held marketing roles at Walmart/Sam's Club, UpWork, and Intuit. This promotion raises questions about the roles of current regional CMOs, including Andrea Stairs, Eve Williams, and Amrita Blickstead, with some executive shuffles already reported in the UK and Australia.

title: EBay’s focus on luxury to refurbished goods drives results beat
summary: EBay Inc. surpassed Wall Street's quarterly targets as inflation-conscious shoppers increasingly bought refurbished goods and the company expanded its luxury offerings. Despite forecasting holiday-quarter revenue slightly below estimates, investors drove shares up nearly 8% in extended trading, recognizing eBay's appeal for value in a high-inflation environment. The company acknowledged risks from a strong U.S. dollar, Europe's energy crisis, and consumer confidence but noted increased demand for home energy products in Germany.

title: EBay modernizes desktop seller tech stack, eliminates legacy tools
summary: eBay has modernized its desktop seller tech stack by migrating all desktop sellers to a single unified listing experience globally, eliminating multiple legacy tools and accelerating innovation. The company also made strategic acquisitions, including myFitment and TCGplayer, to enhance its offerings for collector items and expand its capabilities in areas like inventory management and cross-border trade. These efforts reflect a focus on improving customer experience and driving site-wide conversion, despite a slight decline in net revenue for the quarter.

title: eBay Inc. Announces Pricing $1.15 Billion Senior Unsecured Notes Offering
summary: eBay Inc. announced the pricing of a $1.15 billion underwritten public offering of senior unsecured notes, consisting of 5.900% Notes due 2025, 5.950% Notes due 2027, and 6.300% Notes due 2032. The offering is expected to close on November 22, 2022. eBay intends to use the net proceeds to redeem outstanding floating rate and fixed rate notes due 2023, with the remainder for general corporate purposes.

title: Resale Shoppers to Buy More in 2023, But What About the Seller Side?
summary: A new survey by Morning Consult indicates that the robust resale market is set to grow further in 2023, with 62% of consumers having engaged in purchase-driven resale shopping and 37% expecting to increase their secondhand purchases. While buyer activity is strong across demographics, attracting sellers remains a challenge for companies like TheRealReal. The article highlights the critical imbalance between buyers and sellers, which is driving consolidation in the secondary market.

title: eBay predicts shoppers will ditch buying new products this Black Friday
summary: eBay predicts that shoppers will prioritize pre-loved and refurbished items this Black Friday and holiday season, driven by cost-of-living concerns. Research indicates over half of consumers plan to shop second-hand, potentially saving the nation £8 billion and significantly reducing carbon emissions and waste. eBay plans to exclusively promote refurbished and pre-loved deals this Black Friday, encouraging more sustainable shopping habits.

title: Outfitting Veterans With Pre-Loved Suits to Give Them a Second Chance
summary: Alex Carrera, through his eBay store for the nonprofit "Save a Suit," provides free business attire to veterans nationwide. The eBay store sells pre-loved suits with minor flaws, with all proceeds directly funding the charity's mission to clothe over 5,000 veterans. This initiative not only helps veterans re-enter the workforce but also fosters community connections and demonstrates the profound impact one individual can have.

title: Alibaba, JD.com keep mum on this year’s Singles’ Day transaction volume
summary: For the first time, Chinese e-commerce giants Alibaba and JD.com did not disclose their final gross merchandise volume (GMV) for the Singles' Day shopping festival, amid economic headwinds and China's zero-Covid policy. While Alibaba stated its results were in line with last year's GMV and JD.com claimed "record-breaking" results, analysts speculate the actual figures might have been underwhelming, leading to their decision to withhold specific numbers. This transparency shift reflects the challenging economic environment affecting consumer spending in China.

title: Pitney Bowes Launches PitneyShip™ Cube, the First-of-Its-Kind Shipping Label Printer With Built-In Scale
summary: Pitney Bowes has introduced PitneyShip Cube, a groundbreaking wireless shipping label printer that includes a built-in scale.
