This all-in-one device, combined with the PitneyShip software, enables users to streamline their shipping process by weighing packages, comparing discounted rates, printing labels, and tracking orders from a single compact unit. It is designed to benefit ecommerce sellers and office shippers by saving time and space, enhancing efficiency, and offering discounted USPS and UPS shipping rates.

title: eBay Opens Store in NYC that Accepts Pre-Owned Luxury as Currency
summary: eBay has opened a pop-up "Luxury Exchange" store in New York City's Diamond District, allowing customers to trade their pre-owned luxury jewelry, handbags, and watches for "closet currency" to purchase authenticated items. This initiative responds to the growing trend of luxury resale as an alternative investment, with a recent report indicating that many luxury shoppers view their items as a form of currency. The store highlights eBay's Authenticity Guarantee program, expanding to fine jewelry, and aims to provide an in-person experience reflecting the platform's luxury offerings.

title: eBay Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: On Thursday, November 17, 2022, eBay Inc. stock (EBAY) fell by 0.04% to $45.47, underperforming the broader market indices like the S&P 500 and Dow Jones. Despite this daily loss, eBay outperformed competitors such as Amazon.com Inc., Walmart Inc., and Costco Wholesale Corp., which experienced larger declines. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, with trading volume also below its 50-day average.

title: eBay Motors Auctions One-of-a-Kind Vehicles Collected from a Nationwide Road Trip of America's Hottest Car Cultures
summary: eBay Motors concluded its 'Parts of America' tour in Los Angeles, showcasing eleven custom vehicles collected from diverse car cultures across the nation. Four of these unique vehicles, including a 1969 Ford F100 Ranger and a 2012 Audi R8 Twin-Turbo, are being auctioned on eBay, highlighting the marketplace's role in supporting automotive enthusiasts. This initiative demonstrates how parts from eBay Motors enable the customization and restoration of vehicles, bringing various car cultures to life.

title: Plowing on: Deere & Co. is going from strength to strength
summary: Deere & Co. reported strong sales and profits, forecasting an even better 2023 for its agricultural equipment business. Despite economic concerns, demand for their products (ranging from tractors to specialized mowers) remains robust, with the company successfully passing on cost increases to customers. Deere's shares have outperformed the market, rising 25% this year.

title: Executives from eBay and Golden State Foods Join GS1 US Board of Governors
summary: GS1 US has elected Scott Gaffney from eBay and Brian Dick from Golden State Foods to its Board of Governors. These industry leaders will help guide GS1 US's strategy to enhance supply chain efficiency and visibility across various sectors, supporting members with digital transformation and demand planning. Their expertise is expected to be instrumental in optimizing supply chains to meet consumer needs.

title: Survey: Side Hustlers on the Rise
summary: A LendingTree survey reveals that 44% of Americans now have a side hustle, a 13% increase since 2020, primarily driven by inflation. Many side hustlers, especially those with lower incomes, rely on this extra income to cover primary expenses, earning an average of $473 per month. The study also indicates a recovery in the job market, as fewer people are starting side gigs due to job loss, with inflation being the primary motivator for new side hustles.

title: eBay and Nike honor sneaker legend with charity SB Dunk release
summary: eBay and Nike are commemorating the 20th anniversary of Nike SB with a special charity release of the iconic eBay Dunk sneakers. The original 2003 auction raised funds for skate parks, and this new release honors Sandy Bodecker, Nike's late VP of special projects and a pivotal figure in Nike SB's success, with proceeds benefiting the Sandy Bodecker Foundation. The release includes 10 unique pairs with a skateable shoe box and translucent material, symbolizing cuts made to the original design, and features an event called "Skate 'Em Out the Store" in Portland where popular SB Dunks are sold for $65, provided buyers skate them out.

title: eBay and Nike Reissue the Iconic ‘eBay Dunk’
summary: eBay and Nike are reissuing the iconic "eBay Dunk" to commemorate 20 years of the Nike SB Dunk. This release includes a limited charity auction benefiting The Bodecker Foundation and a general release design. Additionally, the brands are opening a "Skate 'Em Out Store" in Portland, Oregon, offering select Nike SB Dunk shoes at their original 2002 MSRP of $65.

title: App company cannot be held liable for murders of 2 users, judge finds
summary: A federal judge has ruled that the online marketplace app Letgo (now OfferUp) cannot be held liable for the murders of Joseph and Jossline Roland by a user they met through the app. Despite the tragic circumstances, the judge found that the victims' children did not present a plausible claim of liability under Colorado law and that the platform was largely protected by Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act. The ruling highlighted that the app and its users do not have a legal duty to each other in the same way recognized relationships do, and the "verified" label on the killer's profile did not constitute actionable deceptive practice.

title: Nike, eBay reissue iconic 'eBay Dunk' shoe
summary: Nike and eBay have reissued the iconic "SB Dunk" shoe to celebrate its 20th anniversary, with events held in Portland. Special edition shoes were sold and auctioned, with proceeds benefiting the Bodecker Foundation to support arts and skating programs in the local community. This initiative mirrors a 2003 auction that funded skate parks in Portland.

title: eBay launches refurbished Peloton bikes in the UK at £350 off new RRP
summary: eBay UK has launched Certified Refurbished Peloton bikes at a price of £995, offering a saving of £350 off the new RRP. This initiative addresses a 23% surge in demand for Peloton bikes on eBay, providing a more accessible option for fitness enthusiasts. The pre-loved bikes are inspected, refurbished, and tested by Peloton to ensure a like-new condition, backed by a 12-month warranty and eBay's Money Back Guarantee.

title: Barracuda Networks named an eBay exec to be its CFO
summary: Barracuda Networks has appointed Joe Billante, formerly eBay's head of investor relations, as its new Chief Financial Officer. Billante will be responsible for the financial operations of the Campbell-based cybersecurity company. This follows several leadership changes and acquisitions for Barracuda Networks, as previously reported.

title: Will WhatsApp Partnership Boost MercadoLibre’s Earnings?
summary: MercadoLibre's stock has trended lower in 2022, but a new partnership with Meta Platforms' WhatsApp for payment processing in Brazil could provide a significant boost. The e-commerce specialist is testing payment processing for business users on WhatsApp, aiming for a seamless checkout experience. Despite strong earnings and revenue growth, MercadoLibre has recently missed earnings views, and the stock is working on its fifth consecutive week of declines, suggesting it still needs to stage a rally to become a less risky investment.

title: How eBay Employees Are Teaming Up with Nonprofits to Encourage Entrepreneurship
summary: eBay employees are volunteering with nonprofits to foster entrepreneurship in various communities worldwide through the eBay Foundation's Global Give program. This initiative supports aspiring entrepreneurs, particularly within migrant, displaced, and underserved youth populations, providing mentorship, training, and resources. Employee volunteers report that this work enriches their professional and personal lives, demonstrating eBay's commitment to social impact.

title: EBay targets Gen Z with first brand campaign in three years
summary: EBay is launching its first brand campaign in three years, specifically targeting Gen Z. The campaign was announced on December 19, 2022, and aims to connect with a younger demographic. The article is very brief and provides minimal details beyond this announcement.

title: eBay India participates in the 6th edition of Payoneer’s Forum
summary: eBay India participated as an 'export partner' in the 6th edition of the Payoneer Forum, an event series across seven Indian cities aimed at empowering exporters to expand internationally. eBay engaged with cross-border sellers to showcase the ease of international business through its platform, reinforcing its commitment to helping local sellers succeed globally. The forum featured interactive keynotes and masterclasses, with eBay highlighting its Global Xpansion Program and the expansion of its authentication service to the fine jewelry category.

title: eBay Business Model
summary: The eBay business model centers around an online marketplace for buyers and sellers, generating revenue primarily through seller fees, advertising, and subsidiary contributions. Founded in 1995, eBay has grown into a major e-commerce platform by offering a low-fee, accessible auction and direct sales environment. The article details eBay's customer segments, value propositions, revenue streams, key resources, activities, partners, cost structure, and a SWOT analysis, while also comparing it to competitors like Amazon and Alibaba.

title: For Sale on eBay: A Military Database of Fingerprints and Iris Scans (Published 2022)
summary: A German security researcher purchased a military biometric device, a Secure Electronic Enrollment Kit (SEEK II), on eBay for $68. The device, which was last used in Afghanistan in 2012, contained the names, nationalities, photographs, fingerprints, and iris scans of 2,632 individuals, many from Afghanistan and Iraq. This discovery highlights the unintended longevity of military data collection tools and the potential risks if such sensitive information falls into the wrong hands.

title: Top 15 Carvana Competitors & Alternatives in 2023
summary: This article identifies the top 15 competitors and alternatives to Carvana in the used car market, highlighting their business models, services, and unique selling points. It covers online platforms, traditional dealerships, and auction sites, offering a comprehensive overview for consumers looking to buy or sell used cars. The piece details how each alternative compares to Carvana, focusing on features like financing, return policies, and inventory.

title: Why celebrities are investing in online luxury watch reseller Bezel
summary: Online luxury watch reseller Bezel, launched in 2022, has attracted significant investment from celebrities like Kevin Hart, John Legend, and prominent athletes. The company aims to capture a share of the expanding US$20 billion secondary watch market by prioritizing authenticity verification through experts at its Los Angeles headquarters. Bezel CEO Quaid Walker highlights the company's focus on building trust with customers, differentiating its approach from competitors that may lack robust authentication processes.

title: Samsung hires ex-Yogiyo CEO to lead online sales as rivals axe jobs
summary: Samsung Electronics has appointed Kang Shin-bong, former CEO of Yogiyo, as its new online sales chief to enhance direct-to-consumer channels. This move comes as other major tech companies are implementing significant job cuts. Simultaneously, Samsung is actively recruiting semiconductor talent globally through recruitment fairs and partnerships with universities, emphasizing its continued investment in R&D and skilled professionals despite cost-cutting measures.

title: Steve Cohen-Backed Collectibles Firm Goldin Starts New Platform
summary: Goldin, a high-end collectibles auction house backed by Steve Cohen, is launching a new online marketplace to compete with platforms like EBay. The platform offers e-commerce capabilities, authentication, grading services, and a physical vault for storage, aiming to simplify the selling process for collectibles. This move signifies Goldin's strategy to broaden its reach beyond high-end auctions and enter the mainstream market.

title: Mets Owner-backed Marketplace Aims to Rival eBay Collectibles
summary: Goldin Auctions has launched Goldin Marketplace, a new platform for buying and selling collectibles, backed by New York Mets owner Steve Cohen and Collectors Holdings, Inc. The marketplace aims to compete directly with eBay and PWCC, offering integrated authentication, grading services from PSA, and the Collectors Vault. Goldin Marketplace seeks to provide a seamless experience for collectors to research, grade, buy, sell, vault, and insure items, with initial seller fees at 16.7% for items under $2,499 (introductory rate of 8.3%).

title: The Weekly Closeout: Foot Locker faces privacy suit and Levi’s misquotes Mark Twain
summary: This article covers Foot Locker facing a class-action privacy lawsuit under California's Invasion of Privacy Act, seeking $25 million in damages for allegedly recording chat communications without consent. It also highlights Levi's CEO Chip Bergh's statement on skinny jeans, where he misquoted Mark Twain while asserting their continued popularity. The piece further details retail news including 1-800-Flowers launching a marketplace, eBay investing in luxury resale platform Cudoni, and Lowe's introducing kids' birthday party packages.

title: Salesforce appoints new board directors amid activist investor pressure
summary: Salesforce has appointed three new board directors, including hedge fund ValueAct Capital's chief, Mason Morfit, Mastercard CFO Sachin Mehra, and former Carnival Corp CEO Arnold Donald. This move comes amidst increasing pressure from activist investors seeking better cost control and management changes at the cloud-based software firm. While these appointments were planned to enhance operational skills, they also respond to demands from prominent activist investment firms like Elliott Management, Starboard Value, Inclusive Capital, and ValueAct, who have all engaged with Salesforce regarding their concerns.

title: AMZN Earnings: All you need to know about Amazon’s Q4 2022 earnings results
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) reported its Q4 2022 earnings, with net sales increasing 9% year-over-year to $149.2 billion, beating expectations. However, net income significantly dropped to $0.3 billion, or $0.03 per share, from $14.3 billion in the prior year, causing the stock to fall 5% in after-market hours. For Q1 2023, Amazon projects net sales between $121-126 billion.

title: EBay, Notable Live partner on NFTs, experiences for sports fans
summary: EBay has partnered with Notable Live, a digital experience platform, to offer NFTs, exclusive athlete merchandise, and unique experiences to sports fans. This collaboration, backed by an undisclosed investment from EBay Ventures, aims to enhance the retail platform's collectible offerings and connect live experiences with shopping for sports memorabilia. EBay has been expanding its digital collectibles business through initiatives like its authenticity guarantee program for trading cards and acquisitions like NFT marketplace KnownOrigin and collectibles company TCGplayer.

title: eBay Mistakenly Issues 1099-Ks To Individual Seller Names, Not LLCs
summary: eBay has mistakenly issued 1099-K tax forms to individual seller names and SSNs instead of their LLC business names and EINs, causing confusion and frustration among sellers. The issue stems from an outdated account registration process that lumped sole proprietors and LLCs together, preventing existing LLCs from properly having their EIN on the forms. Despite initial incorrect information from customer support, eBay community staff have acknowledged the problem, but a clear resolution for affected sellers is still pending.

title: San Jose-based eBay announces layoff
summary: San Jose-based eBay announced it will cut 4% of its workforce, or approximately 500 employees globally, due to the global macroeconomic environment. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that this restructuring allows the company to invest in high-potential areas like new technologies and customer innovations. Employees affected by the layoffs will receive transition packages including severance; this announcement came on the same day Zoom, another San Jose-based tech company, also announced significant layoffs.

title: President and CEO Dan Schulman Announces Intention to Retire from PayPal at Year-End
summary: PayPal President and CEO Dan Schulman has announced his intention to retire from the company at the end of 2023, with the board initiating a search for his successor. Schulman, who joined PayPal in 2014, is credited with significantly growing the company's financial performance, including increasing revenues from $9.2 billion to $27.5 billion and expanding active accounts to over 430 million. His tenure also saw the implementation of a groundbreaking employee financial wellness program and investments in closing the racial wealth gap.

title: EBay lays off 500 workers
summary: EBay is laying off about 500 employees internationally, representing 4% of its workforce, a move CEO Jamie Iannone states is to invest in new technologies and streamline corporate structure. This follows a trend of layoffs in the retail sector, as companies face financial pressures, with eBay's Q3 revenue declining 5% and gross merchandise volumes dropping 11%. Despite these challenges, eBay continues to invest in new technologies like NFTs and digital collectibles, having acquired KnownOrigin and TCGplayer.

title: eBay Acquires 3PM Shield to Bring Advanced Marketplace Compliance Technology In-House
summary: eBay has acquired 3PM Shield, a provider of AI-based marketplace compliance solutions, to enhance its monitoring capabilities and prevent the sale of counterfeit, unsafe, and illegal items. This acquisition, completed on February 9, 2023, is part of eBay's commitment to maintaining a safe and trusted platform for its global community of sellers and buyers. The integration of 3PM Shield's technology will simplify policy compliance for sellers and strengthen eBay's ability to address problematic behavior and items.

title: eBay Motors Launches New Purchase Protections for Auto Parts & Accessories
summary: eBay Motors has introduced "eBay Guaranteed Fit," a new program providing purchase protections for auto parts and accessories. This initiative ensures that shoppers receive the correct parts for their vehicles or get their money back, significantly enhancing confidence for both buyers and sellers on the platform. The program streamlines the process of finding compatible parts and aims to reduce fitment issues, benefiting from eBay's existing Money Back Guarantee.

title: eBay Motors Launches New Purchase Protections for Auto Parts & Accessories
summary: eBay Motors has launched "eBay Guaranteed Fit," a new program offering purchase protections for auto parts and accessories. This program ensures that if a purchased part with the "Fits" compatibility checkmark doesn't fit the buyer's vehicle, eBay Motors will cover the return cost and issue a full refund. This initiative aims to increase buyer confidence and reduce fitment issues for both shoppers and sellers on the platform.

title: What’s Driving Recommerce? New eBay Report Shows Sellers, Buyers Find Financial Value in Pre-Loved Goods
summary: A new eBay report highlights the surging popularity of recommerce, with 90% of buyers having purchased pre-loved goods in the past year. The study, based on surveys of over 11,000 sellers and 7,400 buyers across multiple countries, reveals that financial benefits and increasing sustainability concerns are the primary drivers for both sellers and buyers participating in the circular economy, especially among younger generations. The report also quantifies the positive environmental impact of recommerce, noting significant avoided waste and carbon emissions.

title: Can PDD’s share price maintain its streak following Temu’s debut?
summary: PDD Holdings' ecommerce app, Temu, made its Super Bowl debut as part of an aggressive push into the North American market, following China's Shein with a discount pricing strategy. Temu has seen rapid growth in downloads and gross merchandise value since its US launch, despite facing stiff competition from established players like Amazon, Shein, and AliExpress. The article also examines the performance of relevant ETFs, including the Global X Emerging Markets Internet & E-commerce ETF and the Invesco Golden Dragon China ETF, both of which hold PDD.

title: For eBay’s buyers, sellers, and investors, sustainability still ranks second place to earnings
summary: eBay's recent Recommerce Report highlights the company's efforts in promoting sustainability through reselling goods, noting significant environmental benefits and positive economic impact. However, despite these efforts, both buyers and sellers on the platform prioritize financial incentives over sustainability. This focus on earnings is mirrored by investors, as eBay's share price dropped following a quarterly earnings report that showed below-expected figures and declining active buyers.

title: Etsy climbs, eBay earnings slide in Q4 and ‘22
summary: Etsy reported earnings gains for Q4 and the full year 2022, primarily driven by reengaging buyers and strong holiday performance, despite some declines related to impairment charges. In contrast, eBay experienced an earnings slide for both Q4 and the full year, with a focus on strategic changes like layoffs and international growth. Both companies refrained from issuing full-year guidance for 2023 due to macroeconomic uncertainties.

title: Amazon Launches OEM Auto Parts Store
summary: Amazon has launched a new OEM Parts Shop, building on its existing Amazon Garage, to offer aftermarket replacement and custom parts from various automotive brands including GM, Stellantis, BMW, and Yamaha. This move is seen as a response to the growth of eBay Motors and aims to simplify the automotive parts shopping experience for customers. The new shop provides detailed product information, transparent pricing, and multiple delivery options, allowing customers to easily find parts using vehicle specifications.

title: Amazon's Lagging Performance Vs. eBay Since 2020 Should Continue
summary: The author reiterates a successful spread trade strategy involving going long on eBay (EBAY) and short on Amazon (AMZN), which has performed well since late 2020 and is suggested to continue in 2023. This recommendation is based on the belief that eBay is undervalued despite slow growth, while Amazon remains overvalued, particularly if a recession occurs, due to its slim margins. The article emphasizes that eBay's future value is often misunderstood, while Amazon's valuation is seen as unsustainable under potential economic pressures.

title: eBay Marks Women's History Month with First-of-Its-Kind Sneaker Drop
summary: eBay is launching "From The Collection: Joy Claire", an exclusive sneaker drop to celebrate Women's History Month and the trailblazing career of Joy Claire in the sneaker and streetwear industry. Starting on International Women's Day, 50 pairs of Claire's rare sneakers, including coveted items like Undefeated x Nike Air Max 97s and NOCTA x Hot Step Air Terras, will be auctioned on eBay with proceeds benefiting The Downtown Women’s Center to support women experiencing homelessness. All eligible sneakers will be backed by eBay's Authenticity Guarantee.

title: Oculis Announces US Public Listing on NASDAQ
summary: Oculis, a global biopharmaceutical company, has announced its listing on the Nasdaq Stock Market under the ticker "OCS" after successfully closing a business combination with European Biotech Acquisition Corp. (EBAC). The company secured approximately $91 million in an upsized PIPE and private investment financing, and its shares began trading on Nasdaq on March 3, 2023. Oculis has a pro-forma enterprise value of about $220 million and over $117 million in cash, aiming to advance its clinical-stage pipeline targeting retina, dry eye, and neuro-ophthalmic diseases.

title: eBay just created a shortcut for building accessible websites
summary: eBay has launched an open-source Figma plugin called "Include" to help designers build more accessible digital tools from the very start of product development. This tool allows designers and engineers to check for accessibility concerns like screen reader compatibility, color scheme readability, and appropriate clickable element sizes, integrating accessibility into the core design process rather than as a late-stage compliance check. Include simplifies the creation of accessible websites for people with disabilities, addressing the issue that a high percentage of websites currently lack accessible experiences.

title: EBay slams 'alarming' Meta subpoena in FTC monopoly case
summary: E-commerce platform eBay has asked a U.S. judge to block a subpoena from Meta Platforms, calling it "alarming" and too broad. Meta is seeking confidential business information from eBay to defend itself against an antitrust lawsuit filed by the Federal Trade Commission. eBay argues that the requested information is not relevant to the FTC's monopoly claims and that it does not compete with Meta in social networking, only with Facebook Marketplace.

title: eBay Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock fell by 0.85% on Wednesday, closing at $40.81, and underperformed the broader market, which also saw declines in the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The company's shares are currently $19.58 below their 52-week high. Competitors such as Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) and Walmart Inc. (WMT) experienced gains on the same day.

title: Etsy, eBay, PayPal Want IRS 1099-K Relief for Online Sellers
summary: Companies like eBay, Etsy, and PayPal are advocating for Congress to increase the reporting threshold for IRS Form 1099-K, which currently stands at $600. They argue that the low threshold disproportionately burdens "casual sellers" and causes confusion, with many transactions for used goods not creating tax liability. A bill, the Saving Gig Economy Taxpayers Act, has been reintroduced in Congress to restore the previous $20,000/200 transaction threshold.

title: Millions of Aussies turning passions to profit by investing in collectibles, reveals eBay State of Collectibles Report 2023
summary: A new report by eBay Australia and Deloitte Access Economics shows that over five million Australians are collecting items such as coins, toys, and sneakers, with more than 40% having a financial objective. Many collectors are turning their hobbies into profit, with median estimated gains of $5,000-$10,000 over the past three years. The report highlights significant price increases in luxury items, trading cards, and LEGO sets, indicating a growing market for collectibles that offers both passion and potential financial returns.

title: IRS to crack down on income earned by third-party sellers on eBay, Amazon and other sites
summary: The IRS is changing its reporting rules for third-party sellers on platforms like eBay and Amazon, requiring companies to issue 1099 forms for transactions exceeding $600, eliminating the previous $20,000 sales threshold. This move aims to ensure that income from online sales is properly taxed. The article highlights a case where an individual was wrongly accused of undeclared sales, demonstrating potential identity theft issues that sellers might face.

title: Investigation into eBay continues after stalking scandal | 60 Minutes
summary: 60 Minutes investigates the stalking and harassment campaign orchestrated by eBay employees against Ina and David Steiner, publishers of EcommerceBytes. This deep dive reveals how eBay executives, unhappy with critical coverage, used company resources to terrorize the couple with disturbing deliveries and threats. While several employees were convicted, the broader investigation into eBay and its top executives continues, raising questions about corporate accountability.

title: Salesforce avoids proxy fight with Elliott after improved results
summary: Salesforce Inc. and activist investor Elliott Management have reached an agreement where Elliott will not pursue a board challenge, following Salesforce's improved financial results and commitment to boosting profits and efficiency. Salesforce agreed to double its share buybacks to $20 billion and reduce acquisitions, satisfying Elliott and avoiding a costly proxy contest. This resolution is part of a trend among prominent activist investors, with similar withdrawals seen at Disney and Bath & Body Works.

title: Kohl’s moves digital marketplace from pilot to full launch
summary: Kohl’s has officially launched its new Kohl’s Marketplace after a successful pilot. This platform allows Kohl’s to expand its e-commerce assortment with thousands of products from third-party sellers across various categories, integrated seamlessly into Kohls.com. The move follows a trend of other major retailers, such as Macy's and The Michaels Companies, also adopting third-party online marketplaces to broaden their offerings.

title: TQQQ: More Risk Than Reward In 2023
summary: The article argues that the ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (TQQQ) carries more risk than reward in 2023, especially if the "Big Tech 2.0" boom has ended. It highlights the detrimental effect of daily decay from futures and swaps in flat or down markets, like 2022. The author suggests that a looming recession and persistent inflation could lead to Big Tech names languishing for a couple more years, making TQQQ a potentially disappointing investment.

title: eBay Australia expands Authenticity Guarantee to luxury wallets
summary: eBay Australia has extended its Authenticity Guarantee service to include eligible luxury wallets priced over $350, covering top brands like Chanel, Dior, and Louis Vuitton. This expansion aims to enhance consumer confidence, accessibility, and sustainability in the pre-loved luxury market. The service verifies items at no extra cost to buyers or sellers, and eBay is also adding over ten new luxury brands to its Authenticity Guarantee for handbags.

title: Real Heat for Mini Feet: Sneakers the new modern heirloom?
summary: eBay UK is expanding its 'Authenticity Guarantee' program to include children's sneakers over £100, driven by a significant increase in parental purchases of branded kids' sneakers. The initiative aims to provide authentication for these valuable items, recognizing the growing market for children's designer footwear and the desire for quality assurance among parents. This expansion reinforces eBay's commitment to becoming a trusted marketplace for high-value goods, potentially positioning sneakers as modern family heirlooms.

title: EBay introduces ‘Certified by Brand’ luxury resale program
summary: EBay has launched its "Certified by Brand" luxury resale program, allowing brands to authenticate secondhand luxury products directly on the platform. The program includes "Direct from Brand" and "Brand-Authorized Seller" categories for items like handbags, jewelry, and timepieces. This initiative aims to address consumer skepticism regarding authenticity in the resale market, providing brands with control over their image and access to market insights.

title: Pre-loved fashion takes centre stage: eBay reveals fashion is being redefined with a growing trend towards pre-loved
summary: eBay Australia's research indicates that pre-loved fashion is becoming a mainstream trend, with a significant number of Australians incorporating it into their wardrobes and increasing their purchases in the last two years. The desire to find unique items, save money, express individuality, and support the circular economy are key drivers. eBay Australia emphasizes the accessibility and sustainability of pre-loved luxury items, highlighting their Authenticity Guarantee for high-value purchases.

title: eBay Motors Expands Installation Services for Tire Buyers
summary: eBay Motors has expanded its tire installation services, making it easier for buyers to purchase tires and have them professionally installed. Over 75% of tires on the platform are now eligible for this service, which allows users to book installation at over 5,000 locations nationwide, including partners like CarAdvise, SimpleTire, Firestone, and PepBoys. The company is also offering free installation through July 2nd to promote the new service.

title: EBay creates event series for sports trading card collectors
summary: EBay has launched "Collectors Camp," an event series for sports trading card enthusiasts to learn about enhancing their collections. The initiative, guided by eBay seller David Prince and NBA player Mikal Bridges, will teach attendees how to use eBay's tools for buying, trading, and selling cards. This program is part of eBay's broader strategy to expand its sports memorabilia and collectibles market, which includes past efforts in authentication, the eBay Vault, and the acquisition of TCGplayer.

title: eBay acquires AI-powered product authentication company Certilogo
summary: eBay has acquired Certilogo, an AI-powered company specializing in digital IDs and authentication for apparel and fashion goods. This acquisition aims to enhance secondhand fashion authentication on eBay's marketplace and solidify its position as a trusted platform for pre-loved items. Michele Casucci, Certilogo's CEO, will continue to lead the Milan-based company.

title: eBay Acquires Certilogo
summary: eBay has acquired Certilogo, a Milan-based provider of AI-powered digital IDs and authentication for apparel and fashion goods. This acquisition aims to enhance eBay's fashion category by integrating "secure by design" digital ID technology, allowing brands to protect against counterfeits and engage in recommerce through digital product passports. Certilogo's CEO, Michele Casucci, will continue to lead the company, which will contribute to expanding opportunities in the circular economy for both consumers and brands.

title: Sriracha bottles selling as high as $80 on eBay, Amazon amid shortage
summary: A shortage of Sriracha hot sauce, caused by a scarcity of chili peppers, has led to significantly inflated prices for the product on various online marketplaces. Unopened bottles are selling for $30 to $80, with some listings fetching up to 10 times their original value as consumers resell their stock. This shortage largely affects Huy Fong Food Inc.'s Sriracha sauces.

title: An End to Regulatory Risk in China’s Internet Sector
summary: Recent meetings between Premier Li Qiang and heads of major Chinese internet companies signal an end to a period of regulatory crackdown in the sector, turning a significant investor headwind into a tailwind. This shift, marked by commitments to transparency and growth support, is seen as paving the way for more sustainable development in China’s internet economy. Investors are looking to capture this potential growth through instruments like the KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (KWEB), which tracks key Chinese tech companies.

title: Time to Buy the Selloff in These Stocks After Earnings?
summary: This article examines three companies—Crocs (CROX), eBay (EBAY), and LendingTree (TREE)—whose stocks fell following their Q2 earnings reports, suggesting these selloffs might be overreactions. Despite some concerns like slowing growth for Crocs' HeyDude brand or declining active buyers for eBay, all three companies largely surpassed earnings expectations. The author suggests these stocks could present buying opportunities as the market adjusts.

title: Inside The RealReal’s ambitious turnaround strategy
summary: The RealReal, a luxury consignment platform, is implementing a three-part turnaround strategy under CEO John Koryl to achieve profitability after years of losses since going public. The plan involves launching an ad network, shifting its inventory focus by reducing low-value items and emphasizing jewelry/accessories, and relying more on third-party sellers to mitigate warehousing costs and increase supply. This strategy aims to leverage The RealReal's audience and meet high demand for vintage fashion while addressing its looming debt in 2025.

title: This Vintage Lover Flew Across the Country to Meet Her Favorite Seller
summary: Emily O’Brien, a 28-year-old vintage clothing collector and seller, went viral on TikTok for documenting her cross-country trip from California to rural Pennsylvania to visit the warehouse of her favorite eBay vintage seller, bydenise. Emily, whose wardrobe is almost entirely vintage, embraces secondhand fashion as a sustainable alternative to fast fashion, a sentiment shared by many of her millennial and Gen Z peers. Her visit led to a deeper connection with the sellers and allowed her to acquire unique pieces for her own collection and business.

title: Nine million and counting: The controversial shopping app sweeping Australia
summary: Temu, a Chinese-owned online marketplace, has rapidly become Australia's fastest-growing shopping app, amassing over 9.2 million users since its April launch. Despite its popularity driven by low prices and strong advertising, Temu faces controversy regarding alleged forced labor, counterfeit goods, and a lack of financial transparency from its parent company, PDD Holdings. The article highlights Temu's significant market presence and the ethical questions surrounding its operations.

title: eBay Hiring Labor Relations Director In Wake Of TCGUnion Win
summary: eBay is seeking to hire a Director of Labor Relations following the TCGPlayer union's successful organizing efforts and subsequent recognition. This move comes as eBay removed language supporting workers' rights to organize from its Human Rights Policy, raising concerns about its commitment to good-faith bargaining. The company has also faced scrutiny regarding its handling of the TCGPlayer acquisition and the potential for further unionization in its labor-centric authentication operations.

title: Spotlighting Our 2023 Global Give Grantees
summary: The eBay Foundation announced its 2023 Global Give grantees, distributing nearly $3 million to 32 nonprofits globally. This program supports organizations that remove barriers to entrepreneurship for historically excluded groups. Recipients like Start Out, Social Entrepreneurs Ireland, and ETIC are empowering diverse entrepreneurs around the world to start and grow their businesses.

title: AMD's Limited Edition Starfield Ryzen 7800X3D and Radeon RX 7900 XTX combo is on eBay
summary: A limited edition Starfield-themed AMD Ryzen 7800X3D CPU and Radeon RX 7900 XTX GPU bundle, one of only 500 produced, is currently being auctioned on eBay. The auction's proceeds will benefit Mental Drive Inc., a nonprofit dedicated to mental health awareness. The custom Radeon RX 7900 XTX features a stunning Starfield design, while the Ryzen CPU comes in a themed box.

title: How One Seller Turned His Love of Cars Into a Business
summary: James Patrick, an eBay seller, transformed his passion for cars and racing into a successful business selling auto parts. Starting from selling his own car on eBay, he recognized the platform's potential and scaled his operation from bookshelves to a warehouse. His business, "Business Bushido," focuses on sourcing parts to help fellow car enthusiasts complete their builds, driven by a deep engagement with car culture and community.

title: eBay Launches Luxury Consignment
summary: eBay has launched a new luxury consignment service, initially for designer handbags, which will expand to include jewelry and watches next year. This service allows users to sell luxury items through expert sellers like Linda's Stuff, with eBay handling authentication and listing details. The initiative builds on eBay's efforts to become a trusted destination for luxury goods, integrating with services like Authenticity Guarantee and Certified By Brand.

title: eBay and ‘Air Fryer King’ Nathan Anthony set to launch the UK’s First Ever Air Fryer Restaurant this September
summary: eBay is partnering with "Air Fryer King" Nathan Anthony to open the UK's first pop-up air fryer restaurant in September. The initiative aims to highlight the value and energy efficiency of refurbished air fryers, especially as Britons face rising energy costs. The restaurant will feature a menu cooked entirely with refurbished appliances, encouraging consumers to consider cost-saving and environmentally friendly refurbished technology available on eBay.

title: Justice Department sues eBay over unlawful sales of pesticides, other products
summary: The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has sued eBay, alleging the online retail giant illegally sold hundreds of thousands of harmful pesticides and other unsafe products, violating environmental laws. This complaint, filed on behalf of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), states that eBay sold unregistered pesticides, products falsely claiming protection against COVID-19, items containing methyl chloride, and "aftermarket defeat devices" that increase vehicle emissions. eBay denies the allegations, stating it dedicates significant resources and technology to prevent such listings and actively partners with law enforcement.

title: Connecting People and Building Communities to Create Economic Opportunity for All
summary: eBay's second annual Small Business Report highlights how the platform supports its sellers, emphasizing partnership over competition. The report, based on a survey of 4,334 sellers across six countries, reveals that a high percentage of sellers attribute their success and growth to eBay, with many identifying as "accidental entrepreneurs" who turned passions into businesses. eBay's global marketplace is also crucial for sellers, enabling them to reach customers in multiple countries.

title: Jeff Staple Auctions Sneakers, Streetwear & Collectibles from His Personal Collection on eBay
summary: Jeff Staple is auctioning exclusive sneakers, streetwear, and collectibles from his personal archives on eBay as part of the "From the Collection" series. Items include rare sneakers like the Bamboo Box Edition Pigeon Panda Dunks, with all proceeds benefiting Art Start and Welcome to Chinatown. This event highlights eBay's role as a marketplace for unique items and Staple's significant influence on sneaker and streetwear culture.

title: Justice Department targets EBay for alleged unlawful sales of pesticides and other toxins
summary: The U.S. Justice Department has filed a civil complaint against EBay, alleging the online marketplace unlawfully sold and distributed hundreds of thousands of products, including unregistered pesticides and motor vehicle emission-evading devices. EBay claims the government's action is unprecedented and states it actively blocks and removes prohibited items, but the Justice Department highlights the environmental and health risks posed by these sales. This action follows previous orders from the EPA against EBay and a past settlement with Amazon for similar violations.

title: Home Depot Tracked a Crime Ring and Found an Unusual Suspect
summary: Home Depot and Florida law enforcement busted an organized retail crime ring led by Robert Dell, a pastor who ran a drug recovery program. Dell allegedly instructed individuals in his program to steal tools from Home Depot stores, which he then resold online for profit. The operation, which involved $3 million in stolen goods sold since 2016, resulted in Dell and four others being arrested and charged with racketeering and dealing in stolen property.

title: Inside the Future of Fashion with eBay’s Pre-Loved Style Director
summary: Amy Bannerman, eBay's pre-loved style director and celebrity stylist, is featured in the BBC mini-documentary series "Fashion Redressed," highlighting her expertise in sustainable fashion and sourcing pre-loved items from eBay. She emphasizes the importance of saving money, finding unique pieces, and promoting value and trust in the fashion industry. Bannerman, joined by Tasha Ghouri, showcases her personal and professional pre-loved wardrobe finds, including an Authenticity Guaranteed luxury handbag.

title: On Amazon, eBay, and Shopify, AI is the new third-party seller
summary: Amazon, eBay, and Shopify are actively integrating generative AI tools to assist third-party sellers with tasks like creating product listings. These AI capabilities aim to simplify labor-intensive duties, such as writing compelling product descriptions and titles, thereby boosting sales and reducing effort for business owners. Beyond listings, AI is expanding into data analytics, customer review analysis, and marketing campaign generation, offering comprehensive support for e-commerce entrepreneurs.

title: Blackstone names Koramco preferred bidder for $553 mn landmark
summary: Blackstone Inc. has chosen South Korea’s Koramco REITs Management and Trust Co. as the preferred bidder for the Arc Place office building in Seoul's Gangnam district, with the sale expected to reach 750 billion won ($552.7 million). This transaction is anticipated to be the largest real estate deal in Korea this year and could revitalize the local office market. Blackstone had acquired the property in 2016 for 470 billion won.

title: QQQJ Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF)
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings for the Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF (QQQJ), including company names, current prices, weight, and shares held. It also breaks down QQQJ's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures. The ETF focuses on publicly traded companies within North America, with a significant allocation to technology and healthcare sectors.

title: Buyout Group Reevaluates Its Pursuit of EBay-Backed Adevinta
summary: A consortium led by Permira and Blackstone Inc. is reconsidering its potential buyout of European online classifieds company Adevinta ASA. The reevaluation comes after Adevinta shares fell significantly and the market outlook has worsened since the initial bid emerged. This decision threatens one of the year's largest potential buyouts.

title: It’s these sellers’ favorite holiday — Halloween!
summary: This article highlights two eBay sellers, Zach Gosciminski and Cherie Harrison, who have found significant success by specializing in Halloween-themed products. Both sellers share their strategies, from early sourcing and managing supply chains to building relationships with manufacturers, to meet year-round demand for costumes and decor on eBay. Their stories illustrate how a niche market like Halloween can lead to a thriving e-commerce business.

title: How One Seller Turned a Skateboard Light Into a Global Business
summary: Glenn Miller of Whiskertin, an Akron, Ohio-based custom lighting business, found global success selling unique lamps, particularly those made from skateboards, on eBay. After participating in eBay's Retail Revival program in 2018, his business expanded internationally, allowing him to grow his showroom and staff. Miller emphasizes the importance of quality craftsmanship, personalized items, and strong online selling practices for his widespread appeal.

title: Blackstone, Permira Near €14 Billion Deal for eBay-Backed Adevinta
summary: A private equity consortium led by Permira and Blackstone Inc. is close to acquiring Adevinta ASA, an eBay-backed European online classifieds company, for an estimated €14 billion ($15 billion) including debt. The offer of 115 kroner per share represents a 34% premium to Adevinta's closing price on September 21, and the consortium has stated it is their final offer for one of the year's largest buyouts.

title: Blackstone and Permira lead $13 bln bid for Adevinta
summary: A private equity consortium led by Permira and Blackstone is planning to acquire eBay-backed online classifieds group Adevinta ASA for approximately 141 billion Norwegian crowns ($13.1 billion). The offer represents a 52.6% premium over the volume-weighted average price before the offer became public, though Adevinta's board could not recommend it. The deal, which has support from existing shareholders like Schibsted and eBay, is one of Europe's largest buyouts this year and is expected to close in Q2 2024.

title: This Seller Turned His Love of Rare Records Into a Business
summary: Alfred Howard, a musician for over 20 years, transitioned into a successful eBay seller of rare vinyl records during the pandemic. His passion for music and record collecting, cultivated since childhood, allowed him to create a thriving online business, providing financial freedom and a flexible lifestyle to spend time with his daughter. He shares insights on record sourcing, selling strategies, and the vibrant community surrounding vinyl collecting on eBay.

title: Nectar360 and American Express renew longstanding co-branded credit card partnership
summary: Nectar360 and American Express have renewed their co-branded credit card partnership, extending an 18-year relationship that provides significant value to Cardmembers. The American Express Nectar Credit Card enables users to collect points on their spending, particularly with Nectar-affiliated brands like Sainsbury's and British Airways, offering enhanced rewards and benefits. This renewal highlights the success of the collaboration in driving value for customers through loyalty points and personalised rewards.

title: eBay Announces Commercial Agreement and Investment in Sports Trading Card Company COMC, Strengthening Commitment to Collectible Enthusiasts
summary: eBay Inc. has announced a commercial agreement and investment in COMC, a sports trading card company, to accelerate the growth of the trading card hobby and enhance the experience for collectors and sellers. This partnership aims to leverage COMC's technology-based listing and management model to offer extensive selection and improve listing capabilities on eBay. The collaboration emphasizes both companies' commitment to innovation within the collectibles market.

title: eBay Dropped From Nasdaq 100 Amidst Increasing Competition & Macroeconomic Headwinds
summary: eBay is among six stocks being removed from the Nasdaq 100 Index, effective December 18, 2023, due to increasing competition from Amazon and macroeconomic headwinds. The annual reconstitution of the index sees eBay and others replaced by new companies, reflecting shifts in market capitalization and volume criteria. This removal highlights eBay's shrinking footprint and an uncertain long-term business outlook.

title: Sell Alibaba (BABA), Buy Pinduoduo (PDD)?
summary: Pinduoduo (PDD) has surpassed Alibaba (BABA) to become China's most valuable e-commerce company, prompting reevaluation of both stocks. While Alibaba's financials are stable despite its stock price decline, PDD has shown stronger growth and profitability, particularly with its global expansion via Temu. The author remains skeptical about wholeheartedly recommending either due to Alibaba's unclear growth path and concerns about the long-term sustainability of PDD's low-price, group-buying model and geopolitical risks.

title: EBay pushes luxury consignment service with curated Jenna Lyons collection
summary: EBay is expanding its luxury consignment services with "The Luxe Line," a mobile consignment concierge bus featuring a curated collection from former J. Crew exec Jenna Lyons. The service allows customers to get luxury handbags appraised and consigned, and shop for high-end accessories. This initiative builds on eBay's previous authentication efforts, including its Certified by Brand program and a streetwear pop-up shop, aiming to simplify selling pre-owned luxury items and attract more luxury consumers.

title: RDE, Inc. (Restaurant.com) Completes Acquisition of CardCash.com, Creating $100 Million+ Incentives Business
summary: RDE, Inc. (Restaurant.com) has announced the completion of its acquisition of CardCash.com, creating a combined incentives business with over $100 million in revenue in 2023. This acquisition significantly expands RDE's cross-selling opportunities and offers Restaurant.com customers a wider range of discounted gift card options across various industries. The integration is expected to provide customers with unprecedented savings on gift card purchases, leveraging digital technology for instant exchange or access to discounted gift cards.

title: eBay to pay $3M after employees sent live spiders, cockroaches to couple
summary: eBay has been ordered to pay $3 million after its employees engaged in a harassment and intimidation campaign against a Massachusetts couple. The campaign involved sending live spiders, cockroaches, and other disturbing items, triggered by the couple's online newsletter coverage of eBay. The company entered into a deferred prosecution agreement, which could lead to charges being dismissed if conditions are met.

title: eBay to Pay $3M After Employees Sent Spiders, Cockroaches to Couple
summary: Online retailer eBay has agreed to pay a $3 million fine to resolve criminal charges related to a harassment campaign waged by employees against a Massachusetts couple. The employees sent disturbing items, including live spiders and cockroaches, to the couple who ran an online newsletter critical of eBay. The company entered a deferred prosecution agreement, taking responsibility for the harassment and intimidation, with an independent monitor overseeing compliance for three years.

title: EBay to pay $3 million penalty over harassment of couple behind newsletter
summary: EBay has agreed to pay a $3 million penalty to resolve a U.S. criminal probe into a harassment campaign orchestrated by its employees against a Massachusetts couple who published an online newsletter critical of the company. The campaign involved sending disturbing items to the couple's home, and federal prosecutors charged eBay with six counts, including stalking and obstruction of justice. EBay also agreed to retain an independent corporate compliance monitor for three years and make changes to its compliance program.

title: Etsy Gift Mode AI Recommendation Tool Launches In Stealth Mode
summary: Etsy has launched "Gift Mode," an AI-powered tool using OpenAI's GPT-4, designed to help shoppers find personalized gift ideas. The new feature, which generates over 200 gift guides, is a significant investment for Etsy, including its first Super Bowl ad. While aiming to make Etsy a year-round gifting destination, initial impressions from sellers highlight some issues with relevance and policy adherence.

title: Building an eBay Store Called ‘Rack ‘N Roll’
summary: The article highlights Colin Wells' journey from selling a vintage pitcher to establishing a successful eBay store, "Rack 'N Roll," specializing in refurbished car rack systems. Wells' business, largely built on repurposing items from a local salvage shop, reflects his commitment to sustainability and work-life balance after leaving a demanding corporate job. The catchy brand name and Scooby-Doo inspired logo have contributed to the store's strong performance, with 80% of sales on eBay and 20% locally in Steamboat Springs, Colorado.

title: eBay cutting 1,000 jobs, will reduce contract workforce
summary: eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 full-time jobs, representing about 9% of its workforce, and will also reduce its contract workforce to become more nimble and efficient. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that the company's headcount and expenses had outpaced business growth, despite strategic progress. This move follows similar workforce reductions by other major e-commerce retailers like Amazon and Wayfair amidst a cooling e-commerce boom.

title: eBay to slash 1,000 jobs, scale back contracts
summary: eBay Inc. announced it will cut approximately 1,000 jobs, representing about 9% of its workforce, and scale back contracts within its alternative workforce. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that the headcount and expenses have outpaced business growth, necessitating organizational changes to improve efficiency and customer experience. This move follows a trend of tech layoffs after pandemic-era hiring booms.

title: eBay Stock Surges After Saying It Will Cut 9% of Its Workforce—Monitor These Key Levels
summary: eBay announced plans to cut 9% of its workforce, or 1,000 jobs, which led to a nearly 3% surge in its stock price. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that the company's headcount and expenses had outpaced business growth. The article also provides key technical levels for investors to monitor eBay's stock performance within a descending channel pattern.

title: eBay announces 9% workforce reduction to streamline operations
summary: eBay announced a 9% workforce reduction, impacting almost 1,000 full-time employees, to better align staffing and expenses with its business growth. The company also plans to scale back its alternate workforce and this marks the second round of job cuts within a year, following a previous reduction in February 2023. This move aims to streamline operations and improve decision-making speed.

title: PayPal Taps Multiple Updates for Its App And Venmo
summary: PayPal is rolling out significant updates to its app and Venmo service to enhance shopping experiences and "future-proof" the company. Key features include PayPal Fastlane for one-click guest checkout, AI-powered Smart Receipts with personalized recommendations, an updated offers platform for merchants, and CashPass for consumer cashback. Venmo business profiles are also getting improvements to spur commercial activity.

title: PayPal pilots AI-based personalization features
summary: PayPal is launching six new AI-based customer engagement solutions throughout 2024 to revolutionize commerce. These features include biometric login, one-click checkout (Fastlane by PayPal), smart receipts with personalized recommendations, an advanced offers platform, mobile app cashback offers (CashPass), and enhanced Venmo business profiles. The goal is to accelerate transactions, personalize shopping experiences, and increase customer and merchant engagement.

title: Online retailer eBay is cutting 1,000 jobs
summary: Online retailer eBay Inc. is cutting approximately 1,000 jobs, or 9% of its workforce, due to its employee numbers and costs exceeding business growth in a slowing economy. CEO Jamie Iannone stated the company needs to reorganize teams for better speed and decision-making. This follows an earlier layoff of 500 employees (4% of its workforce) in February of the previous year.

title: This Disney Pin Collector Made Her Dreams Come True on eBay
summary: Mayumi Roehm, a Disney pin enthusiast, turned her hobby into a successful business, My-D Pins and Collectibles, which operates both in Anaheim, California, and significantly on eBay. She discusses her journey from a passionate collector to a professional trader, sourcing pins from across the U.S., authenticating them, and building a community around her passion. Roehm also shares advice for other sellers on finding and engaging with collectors.

title: eBay Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: eBay Inc. stock declined by 2.10% on Wednesday to $41.07, performing better than the overall market which saw the S&P 500 Index fall by 1.61% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average drop by 0.82%. This marked the stock's third consecutive day of losses.

title: EBay to pay $59 million to settle US charges over illegal pill-making machines
summary: EBay has agreed to pay $59 million and enhance its compliance measures to settle U.S. Department of Justice allegations that it failed to prevent criminals from buying pill-making machines used for counterfeit drugs, including fentanyl-laced pills. This settlement is the first of its kind for an e-commerce company under the federal Controlled Substances Act, addressing eBay's alleged inadequate monitoring since October 2015. EBay denied wrongdoing but settled to avoid litigation costs, committing to reporting on its compliance measures for three years.

title: What companies experienced massive layoffs in 2024? Who's making cuts so far
summary: Several major companies have announced significant layoffs in early 2024, impacting various sectors from technology and retail to healthcare and airlines. Companies like UPS, Microsoft, Humana, eBay, American Airlines, Wayfair, Twitch, Google, Business Insider, and Sports Illustrated are reducing their workforces due to factors such as economic conditions, restructuring efforts, and changes in business strategies. These cuts range from hundreds to thousands of employees globally.

title: eBay Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) shares rose 1.53% on Thursday, closing at $41.70, breaking a three-day losing streak. Despite this gain, the stock underperformed compared to the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average saw larger percentage increases. The positive day for eBay occurred within an overall favorable trading session for the stock market.

title: Decoding eBay’s Turbulent Journey of Challenges and Opportunities in 2024
summary: eBay has navigated significant legal challenges and workforce reductions in early 2024, including settling lawsuits with the DOJ and DEA, and undergoing a 9% workforce layoff. Despite these issues, the company’s financial health is strong, and it continues to be a major e-commerce platform offering substantial opportunities for sellers. CEO Jamie Iannone is leading revitalization efforts focusing on core strengths and adapting to evolving e-commerce trends.

title: Snap, the owner of Snapchat, is laying off about 10% of its global workforce
summary: Snap Inc., the company behind Snapchat, is reducing its global workforce by approximately 10%, impacting about 530 employees. This marks the latest in a series of layoffs for the tech company, with estimated charges of $55 million to $75 million primarily for severance. Snap plans to report its Q4 and full-year financial results soon, joining other tech giants like Microsoft, eBay, and Google in recent workforce reductions.

title: Recommerce for Lovers
summary: This article explores the enduring popularity of vintage valentines on eBay, highlighting sellers like Maria Watson and Robin Ramsey who specialize in antique cards. It delves into the history of Valentine's Day cards, crediting Esther Howland with their mass production in the 19th century, and notes that modern consumers are drawn to the nostalgia, intricate details, and sentimental value of these historical artifacts.

title: Bieber welcomes former Honest Co CEO to Rhode
summary: Hailey Bieber's Rhode Skin has appointed Nick Vlahos, former CEO of Honest Co, as its new CEO. Vlahos is recognized for his instrumental role in Honest Co's growth, including its IPO valued at $2.1 billion, and previously served as COO at Clorox. Bieber stated that Vlahos's expertise in growth, innovation, distribution, global expansion, and team development will be crucial for Rhode's future accelerated growth.

title: Cosmin Pitigoi to Join Flywire as Chief Financial Officer
summary: Flywire Corporation (Nasdaq: FLYW) announced that Cosmin Pitigoi will join the company as Chief Financial Officer, effective March 4, 2024. Pitigoi, a former senior finance executive at PayPal, brings extensive experience in global payments and will oversee various financial functions at Flywire. Current CFO Mike Ellis will transition to a senior advisor role to ensure a smooth handover.

title: eBay Releases 151 Pokémon Collectibles in Ultra-Rare One-Day Auction
summary: eBay is hosting an "Catch 151" auction on February 27th, Pokémon Day, featuring 151 ultra-rare Pokémon cards and collectibles, with starting bids at $1.51. This one-day event offers enthusiasts a chance to acquire iconic pieces, including rare Charizard cards, drawing on eBay’s history as a major destination for Pokémon inventory. The auction also highlights recent trends in Pokémon collecting, including significant increases in searches for specific cards and collaborations.

title: eBay Among Top S&P 500 Gainers With 8% Surge on Buoyant Results, Buyback Plan
summary: eBay's shares surged 8% after reporting better-than-forecast fourth-quarter results, including adjusted EPS of $1.07 and revenue of $2.56 billion. The company also announced an increased quarterly dividend and a $2 billion boost to its share buyback program. Despite competitive pressures and macroeconomic challenges, eBay provided an optimistic outlook for the current quarter, with expected adjusted EPS between $1.19 and $1.23.

title: Famous booth from 'The Sopranos' final episode sells on eBay for $82K
summary: The iconic booth from the final episode of "The Sopranos," located in Holsten's Ice Cream in Bloomfield, N.J., was sold on eBay for $82,600. The owners of Holsten's put the booth up for auction as part of renovations, citing the need for an upgrade due to its age and numerous repairs. The final scene of the show, known for its controversial ending, featured the Soprano family at this diner.

title: eBay Chief Legal Officer Marie Oh Huber Announces Departure
summary: eBay's Chief Legal Officer Marie Oh Huber is departing the company after nearly nine years, with VP & Deputy General Counsel Ken Ebanks serving as interim. Her departure comes amidst increasing legal and regulatory scrutiny on eBay, particularly concerning the 2019 cyberstalking scandal, where internal emails show she was involved in discussions about the target of the harassment. The article highlights concerns about eBay's corporate culture, legal battles, and other regulatory issues that fell under her purview.

title: After Earnings, Is The RealReal Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar believes The RealReal's stock is significantly overvalued despite an increased fair value estimate of $2.08, primarily due to the restructuring of a significant portion of its 2025 convertible notes. While the company achieved its first-ever quarter of positive free cash flow, its poor financial health, high debt burden, and "Very High" uncertainty rating suggest an uphill climb to sustained profitability and solvency. The interest rate on its restructured debt further indicates a continued perceived risk.

title: 15 Best eBay Alternatives to Try in 2024
summary: This article lists the 15 best eBay alternatives for 2024, discussing why eBay is a popular choice for sellers and introducing other major e-commerce platforms like Walmart, Amazon, and Target. It also provides a detailed ranking of 15 alternatives based on site visits, including information on their offerings and seller programs.

title: News | Salesforce Cuts San Francisco Office Footprint by 45% Over Past Year — And It May Not Be Done
summary: Salesforce has significantly reduced its office footprint in San Francisco, cutting its leased or owned space by 45% over the past year to 900,000 square feet. This reduction is part of a broader corporate strategy that includes workforce cuts and offering an additional 2 million square feet for lease. The move reflects the ongoing struggles in San Francisco's office market, which faces high availability rates, although there's some optimism from recent AI company leases.

title: eBay Surveys Buyers On C2C Initiatives - Is "eBay It Forward" Making A Comeback?
summary: eBay is surveying buyers about "Ebay It Forward," a C2C initiative focused on used goods, signaling a potential return to its roots after a period of focusing on "high value, enthusiast buyers." This shift comes amidst flat "enthusiast buyer" figures, recent layoffs, and the departure of Chief Business Strategy Officer Stefanie Jay, suggesting a pivot back to a broader consumer-seller strategy. The company is exploring free selling for private consumers in the UK and Germany, aiming to regain market share from competitors.

title: eBay Motors Drops Collection of Iconic Memorabilia From Ken Block’s Storied Motorsports Career
summary: eBay Motors is launching "From the Collection: Ken Block," an auction of unique memorabilia from Ken Block's motorsports career, with all proceeds benefiting 43 Institute. The auction, starting on Ken Block Day, April 3, features 43 collectible items from his iconic Gymkhana series, rally races, and more, along with limited-run apparel. This initiative honors Block's legacy by supporting aspiring talent in motorsports, action sports, and creative arts.

title: Sherwin-Williams Reveals “The Loneliest Color” with Dapper Dan
summary: Sherwin-Williams has announced "The Loneliest Color" campaign for 2024, highlighting Kingdom Gold (SW 6698), one of its least popular shades. Harlem fashion designer Dapper Dan is the creative director for the project, aligning his past as an industry outlier with the effort to bring an overlooked color into the spotlight. The campaign also includes an eBay auction of a limited-edition capsule collection, with proceeds benefiting Habitat for Humanity and Harlem affiliate organizations.

title: eBay Acquires Goldin From Collectors, Sells eBay Vault To PSA
summary: eBay has announced a series of transactions with Collectors, including acquiring Goldin Auctions and selling its eBay Vault to Collectors' subsidiary PSA. This strategic move aims to create an integrated experience for trading card collectors, offering streamlined grading, storage, and selling services. The deal also raises questions about potential insider knowledge due to a recent hire and increased scrutiny on eBay's M&A practices and ongoing legal issues.

title: eBay acquires Goldin, sells vault to PSA in strategic deals
summary: eBay Inc. has acquired Goldin, a leading trading card auction house, and simultaneously sold its vault to PSA, a prominent authentication and grading provider. These strategic transactions aim to streamline the trading card market by offering a more cohesive buying, selling, grading, and storage experience for collectors in the U.S., with a full product experience expected in the coming months. The moves are intended to expand inventory for eBay customers, provide Goldin sellers with a larger audience, and integrate grading and selling services more seamlessly.

title: eBay & San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan Discuss Shared Priorities & Continued Collaboration
summary: The article reports on a meeting between eBay executives and San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan to discuss shared priorities and collaboration, against a backdrop of eBay's ongoing legal issues and controversies. It highlights past involvement of city officials in eBay's cyberstalking scandal and critiques a sales tax sharing deal between eBay and San Jose. The author questions the nature of the continued collaboration given eBay's troubled history and financial arrangements with the city.

title: eBay Exec Compensation, Compliance & Ethics Changes Revealed In 2024 Proxy Statement
summary: eBay's 2024 Proxy Statement reveals executive compensation, with CEO Jamie Iannone earning over $21.5 million in 2023, and significant changes to its compliance and ethics programs. The company separated internal management of ethics and compliance in 2023, with a new Chief Compliance Officer reporting to the CFO, a change likely driven by negotiations with the DOJ regarding the 2019 cyberstalking case. The article raises questions about the newly appointed Chief Ethics Officer, Aaron Johnson, who was involved in communications related to the cyberstalking incidents, and scrutinizes potential conflicts of interest within eBay's legal and compliance departments, particularly concerning the TCGPlayer acquisition.

title: eBay Launches 5th Annual Up & Running Grants Program, Awarding $500,000 to Small Businesses
summary: eBay is launching its 5th annual Up & Running Grants program, which will award $500,000 to 50 U.S. eBay sellers. Each recipient will receive a $10,000 grant, plus a $500 stipend for technology and continued education, to help grow their small businesses. The program aims to support entrepreneurs on the platform by providing capital and resources for business improvements, inventory, hiring, training, and marketing, and is administered in partnership with Hello Alice.

title: Air taxi Blade is actually an organ transport business in disguise
summary: Blade, initially known for its air taxi services, has surprisingly transformed into America's largest dedicated air transporter of human organs. While a significant portion of its 2023 revenue still came from passenger flights, the majority now stems from transporting critical organs like hearts, livers, and lungs. This strategic shift was driven by delays in eVTOL adoption, the acquisition of Trinity Air Medical, and the essential nature of organ transport providing a stable and growing business segment less susceptible to seasonal fluctuations and regulatory pressures.

title: eBay Inc. Reports First Quarter 2024 Results
summary: eBay Inc. reported strong first-quarter 2024 results, exceeding its outlook with a 2% revenue increase to $2.6 billion and a 1% rise in Gross Merchandise Volume to $18.6 billion. The company returned $638 million to shareholders and highlighted significant progress in innovation, advertising revenue growth, and strategic initiatives like expanding its consignment service for luxury apparel. eBay also provided guidance for the second quarter of 2024, projecting revenues between $2.49 billion and $2.54 billion.

title: eBay Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock fell by 0.76% on Wednesday, closing at $49.69, despite a mixed trading day for the broader market. This performance led to eBay underperforming compared to some of its competitors. The stock closed $3.24 below its 52-week high reached in late March.

title: Collectors and eBay Transactions Close, Summer Launch Expected for Integrated, End-to-End Hobby Experience
summary: Collectors, the parent company of PSA, and eBay Inc. have finalized previously announced transactions, including the sale of Goldin to eBay and the eBay vault to PSA. This partnership aims to launch a new, integrated experience for trading card collectors this summer, streamlining grading, storing, and selling. The collaboration seeks to enhance the collecting journey through trusted transactions, transparent data, and innovative features like real-time tracking and image-based listing capabilities.

title: eBay to streamline card trading following close of Collectors deal
summary: eBay is launching a new, streamlined system for trading cards this summer, following its transactions with Collectors. This initiative involves selling the eBay vault to PSA, acquiring Goldin, and forming a commercial agreement to create a combined trading card vault. The partnership aims to simplify buying, grading, storing, and selling for collectors by integrating eBay's marketplace with PSA's grading services and vault product.

title: eBay Loses VP Communications As Michelle Friedman Moves On To Samsara
summary: Michelle Friedman, eBay's VP Global Communications, has left the company to become VP Communications at Samsara. Friedman joined eBay in September 2020, taking on the role shortly after the 2019 eBay Cyberstalking Scandal became public. Her departure comes as eBay is undergoing a significant strategy shift.

title: eBay Celebrates Recommerce Day With Live Shows For Luxury Pre-Owned Goods
summary: eBay is celebrating its inaugural Recommerce Day with a homepage takeover and livestream events focusing on luxury pre-owned and refurbished goods, featuring sustainability advocate Alicia Silverstone. Concurrently, eBay released its annual Recommerce Report, surveying over 28,000 people globally on recommerce trends. However, some sellers of vintage and collectible items feel excluded by eBay's narrow focus on high-value categories, questioning the event's inclusivity for their businesses, despite eBay surveying buyers on broader C2C initiatives and making recent shifts towards wider consumer-seller focus in other markets.

title: eBay: No Sizzle Except For Value (NASDAQ:EBAY)
summary: eBay's stock has seen a nearly 20% jump year-to-date, yet the company struggles with relevance and achieving substantial growth despite increased marketing spend. Its TTM free cash flow falls short of shareholder returns, with little prospect for significant future FCF growth. The stock's primary appeal lies in its undervaluation at 11x P/E, but its growth stagnation suggests that a discounted multiple is warranted.

title: This Seller Grew His Tech Business Almost 10x in Three Years
summary: Andrew Havens, an eBay Up & Running grantee, scaled his scientific tech business nearly tenfold in three years after pivoting his sales strategy during the pandemic. The grant enabled him to acquire inventory, partner with someone, and optimize his warehouse operations. Havens emphasizes the importance of niche focus, global shipping, automated marketing, and consistently improving processes, advising other sellers to apply for the grant and be authentic in their endeavors.

title: Rakuten, eBay team up to test US market for used Japanese fashion goods
summary: Rakuten Group is partnering with eBay to test the U.S. market for used Japanese fashion goods, leveraging the weak yen and global demand for affordable items. The trial, starting with seven vendors on Rakuten's Rakuma platform, aims to capitalize on Japanese luxury goods' reputation for quality and the cost-of-living squeeze. If successful, the initiative could expand Rakuma's offerings to other eBay markets like Canada, the UK, Germany, and Australia.

title: eBay enables AI-based mobile seller photo enhancement
summary: eBay has launched a new AI-based tool for mobile sellers to enhance product listing photos, allowing them to remove backgrounds and place items in AI-generated backdrops. This feature builds on previous AI tools released by eBay, including generative AI for drafting social media posts and analyzing listing details from images. The company aims to make the listing process more seamless and efficient for sellers to drive sales.

title: Estée Lauder Completes Acquisition of DECIEM
summary: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. has finalized its acquisition of DECIEM Beauty Group Inc., purchasing the remaining interests for approximately US$860 million, bringing the total investment to US$1.7 billion. ELC initially invested in DECIEM in 2017 and became the majority owner in 2021. This acquisition is part of ELC's strategy to leverage DECIEM's innovative, multi-brand approach and transparent skincare solutions to expand its global reach and drive profitability.

title: eBay to Discontinue Acceptance of American Express Payments Starting August 17, 2024
summary: eBay announced its decision to discontinue the acceptance of American Express payments globally starting August 17, 2024. This change is attributed to the "unacceptably high fees" charged by American Express, with eBay calling for more robust regulations to increase competition in the credit card network market. Customers will be notified to transition to alternative payment options available on the marketplace.

title: EBay to drop American Express over fees, says customers have other options
