summary: eBay announced its decision to stop accepting American Express globally starting August 17, citing "unacceptably high fees" for processing credit card transactions. The online marketplace stated that customers now have numerous alternative payment options available. American Express, in response, claims eBay's cost to accept AmEx cards is comparable to other networks and believes the decision is inconsistent with increasing competition.

title: eBay Open 2024 Virtual Seller Conference Scheduled For September 25-26
summary: eBay has scheduled its annual virtual seller conference, eBay Open 2024, for September 25-26. Registration is now open for the event, which will feature keynotes from eBay executives, breakout sessions, seller labs, and networking opportunities. While the event is primarily virtual, over 30 seller-hosted kickoff events are planned for September 24th, with some anticipation around whether eBay will offer more live engagement with executives this year compared to previous pre-recorded sessions.

title: eBay to End American Express Payments Over Interchange Fees
summary: eBay has announced it will no longer accept American Express card payments due to "unacceptably high" interchange fees, citing the availability of numerous alternative payment methods for its customers. This decision, while potentially saving eBay money in the short term, has been criticized by analysts like Brian Riley of Javelin Strategy & Research, who believes it could harm eBay in the long run by alienating high-spending Amex customers. American Express expressed disappointment, stating that eBay's move is inconsistent with increasing competition at the point of sale and that eBay accounts for a very small portion of their total network volume.

title: Chinese Retailer Temu Gains Edge Over eBay In US Market
summary: Chinese e-commerce platform Temu has surpassed eBay in attracting repeat monthly customers in the U.S., despite being a newer entrant. A survey by Omnisend indicates that 34% of consumers shop on Temu monthly compared to eBay's 29%. This growth is attributed to Temu's significant investments in marketing and promotional discounts, including Super Bowl commercials, which have led to a rapid increase in its U.S. market presence.

title: Christine Su believes generative AI will elevate your eBay experience
summary: Christine Su, featured on Fast Company's Queer 50 list, is leading AI-powered recommendation efforts at eBay to improve user experience for both buyers and sellers. Her career path includes addressing ethical challenges in AI and free speech at Twitter (now X) and founding a climate-tech startup, demonstrating her commitment to tackling complex systemic problems. Su emphasizes the importance of diverse perspectives in product creation, particularly given her own experiences as an Asian, nonbinary individual.

title: eBay Quietly Grows China Sales As CEO Downplays Temu Competition
summary: Despite eBay CEO Jamie Iannone publicly downplaying competition from Temu and Shein, eBay has been quietly growing its sales from China. A survey indicates Temu is attracting more repeat buyers than eBay, while eBay's financial reports show significant and growing revenue from China-based sellers, reaching over $1 billion in 2023. This growth, particularly in cross-border trade, suggests a strategy contrasting Iannone's claims of moving away from overseas inventory.

title: Farm Startups Bent on Shaking Up Cargill, ADM Are Stumbling
summary: Startups aimed at disrupting the $1.5 trillion agriculture sector, including major players like Cargill and ADM, are facing significant challenges and stumbling. Companies like Indigo Ag, Farmers Edge, and Gro Intelligence have either shrunk operations, been taken private at massive discounts, or are shutting down entirely. The primary issues include difficulty applying the Silicon Valley model to farming, resistance from established industry giants, a sharp decline in agrifoodtech funding, and farmers' reluctance to share sensitive data with new companies.

title: Etsy Exclusive Shipping Deal Raises Anticompetitive Concerns
summary: Etsy's exclusive shipping deal with ShipEntegra for sellers in Turkey has raised concerns among US businesses, who claim it limits competition and violates antitrust principles. The deal, which makes ShipEntegra the sole shipping service option for certain Turkish sellers, is seen as detrimental to small businesses and American entrepreneurs supporting Turkish artisans. Etsy provided a statement clarifying that the exclusivity applies to new sellers and existing sellers not already using another shipping provider via the Etsy API, while others can continue with legacy providers or use external shipping partners.

title: Seagate Teams Up with eBay to Expand Hard Drive Circularity Program
summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc has partnered with eBay to expand its hard drive circularity program, establishing an official storefront on eBay for factory-recertified hard drives. This initiative aims to reduce e-waste by promoting the secure reuse of storage devices, preventing millions of hard drives from being shredded annually. Seagate's program ensures comprehensive data sanitization and erasure, extending the life of hardware and contributing to sustainability efforts.

title: Press Release: PayPal Names Srini Venkatesan as Chief Technology Officer - Jun 20, 2024
summary: PayPal Holdings, Inc. announced the appointment of Srini Venkatesan as Chief Technology Officer (CTO), effective June 24, 2024. Venkatesan, who joins from Walmart, will lead technology development across the PayPal ecosystem, including AI and machine learning. He will report to PayPal President and CEO Alex Chriss.

title: Elton John Partners with eBay to Release a Personal Collection of Pre-loved Fashion in Support of Elton John AIDS Foundation
summary: Elton John has partnered with eBay to launch "Rocket Man Resale," an auction of hundreds of items from his personal wardrobe to benefit the Elton John AIDS Foundation. The auction, starting June 27, 2024, will feature luxury items like Gucci jackets and Versace robes, with 100% of the proceeds going towards the Foundation's mission to end AIDS. Additionally, other celebrities like David Furnish and Donatella Versace will contribute items, and shoppers can support the Foundation directly through eBay checkout.

title: 2 Top E-Commerce Stock Picks
summary: Despite a challenging economic environment and a slowdown in e-commerce growth post-COVID-19, Morningstar identifies opportunities for investors in online retail. Senior equity analyst Sean Dunlop names Allegro (ALEGF) and Chewy (CHWY) as top picks, alongside Amazon (AMZN), eBay (EBAY), Etsy (ETSY), and MercadoLibre (MELI) as key players benefiting from the long-term shift to online commerce. The article emphasizes investing in companies with strong market dominance and self-reinforcing network effects.

title: eBay's Ken Ebanks Steps In As Interim General Counsel As Marie Oh Huber Exits
summary: Ken Ebanks, eBay's current VP & Deputy General Counsel Markets & Privacy, has been appointed interim General Counsel following Marie Oh Huber's departure. Ebanks, based in Switzerland, takes on this role amidst several ongoing legal challenges for eBay, including the high-profile cyberstalking scandal and various regulatory scrutinies. His extensive experience in M&A may prove beneficial during this tumultuous period for the company.

title: Selling Items Online? Is the IRS Watching?
summary: This article discusses the IRS's increased scrutiny on online sales and payments, particularly concerning the Form 1099-K reporting threshold. It explains how the American Rescue Plan aimed to lower the reporting threshold to $600, but the IRS delayed its implementation. For 2024, the threshold is set at $5,000 with no transaction minimum, differentiating between "business" sales and "hobby" or "garage sales" for tax purposes and advising consultation with a tax attorney.

title: Where does eBay do most of its AI development? You might be surprised
summary: EBay primarily conducts its AI development and training in its own on-premises data centers rather than relying heavily on public cloud resources. This strategy allows the company to maintain high standards of customer privacy and compliance, achieve faster time to market, and leverage its extensive engineering talent. While public cloud is used for seasonal demand spikes, eBay believes its core competency for large-scale AI workloads is best served by its dedicated HPC infrastructure despite challenges like rapidly evolving GPU technology and power/cooling demands.

title: Final ex-eBay employee in Mass. stalking case sentenced
summary: The final ex-eBay employee involved in a cyberstalking campaign against a Massachusetts couple, Brian Gilbert, was sentenced to time served, one year of supervised release, and a $20,000 fine. This sentencing concludes the legal proceedings for the seven former eBay employees and contractors convicted in the scheme. eBay itself was charged criminally and paid a $3 million penalty for its role in the harassment, which involved sending disturbing items and threatening messages to authors of a newsletter critical of the company.

title: EBay seeks judge's guidance to settle with victims of harassment campaign
summary: EBay is seeking court guidance to settle a lawsuit brought by a Massachusetts couple who were victims of a harassment campaign by former eBay employees. The company requested the judge's input on whether the Steiners are entitled to seek punitive damages, which is a key barrier to settlement. This comes as the last of seven former eBay workers involved in the cyberstalking scheme was sentenced.

title: Last person sentenced in eBay harassment scheme involving bloody pig mask
summary: The last person involved in the eBay harassment scheme, Brian Gilbert, a former senior manager of special operations for eBay Inc.'s global security team, was sentenced to time served, one year of supervised release, and a $20,000 fine. This sentencing concludes the prosecution of the cyberstalking campaign orchestrated by seven eBay employees and contractors against a Natick, Massachusetts couple, David and Ina Steiner, who published an online newsletter critical of eBay. The harassment included sending disturbing items like a preserved fetal pig and a bloody pig Halloween mask, and physically surveilling the victims.

title: This Luxury Watch Seller Built a Multi-Million Dollar Business
summary: Arman Baig, a third-generation luxury watch seller, has transformed his family's business into a multi-million dollar venture by successfully expanding onto eBay. His online shop, Swiss Ice, specializes in sourcing unique, on-trend timepieces globally and provides exceptional, personalized customer service. Baig attributes much of his success to leveraging eBay's tools like Authenticity Guarantee, Product Research, and eBay Live events, and plans to continue growing Swiss Ice into a leading online destination for luxury watches.

title: eBay Q2 2024 Earnings - Lowered Forecasts Call Enthusiast Strategy Into Question
summary: eBay's Q2 2024 earnings report reveals stalled Active Buyer stats, minimal GMV growth, and lowered Q3 forecasts, casting doubt on CEO Jamie Iannone's enthusiast buyer strategy. The company is facing challenges with flat enthusiast buyer numbers, unimpressive Q4 expectations, and concerns regarding executive management's role in the platform's decline. Despite some positive initiatives like Venmo integration and business cash advances, overall growth remains sluggish, leading to skepticism about eBay's future trajectory.

title: EBay’s AI investments fuel streamlined experiences for buyers and sellers
summary: EBay is leveraging generative AI to enhance both buyer and seller experiences on its platform. New tools like "Shop the Look" and "Explore" help buyers find and personalize items, while "Magical Listing" streamlines the selling process. These AI investments also extend to customer service operations, aiming to make eBay's marketplace more efficient and user-friendly for all customers.

title: Williams-Sonoma Ranked as Time’s Top Retailer Among Midsize Companies
summary: Williams-Sonoma has been ranked by Time as the top retailer among midsize companies and 17th overall in their 2024 America’s Best Midsize Companies list. The company was recognized for its positive revenue growth, employee satisfaction, and sustainability transparency. Additionally, Arianna Huffington was appointed to Williams-Sonoma's board of directors.

title: ‘Scarcity and growth are oppositional’: How streetwear legend Supreme lost its luster
summary: Streetwear brand Supreme has seen a significant decline in its cultural cachet and financial performance, leading to its sale at a loss by VF Corp to EssilorLuxottica for $1.5 billion. Analysts attribute this decline to an attempt at aggressive expansion and growth by VF Corp, which contradicted Supreme's core value of scarcity and exclusivity. The brand also faces increased competition and a loss of its counterculture appeal due to its corporate affiliations.

title: How Does PayPal Make Money? (2022)
summary: This article details PayPal's diverse revenue streams, including transaction fees for personal and business accounts, Payflow Payment Gateway fees, and "Business in a Box" commission. It also covers revenue from PayPal Working Capital loans, international money transfer fees, withdrawal fees, PayPal Credit interest, PayPal Here fees, and interest earned from deposited money. The company, founded in 1998, has 305 million active accounts and processed $712 billion in total payment volume in 2019, highlighting its broad financial impact.

title: eBay launches major search redesign
summary: eBay has introduced a new search interface following 18 months of research and testing, featuring larger, high-resolution images, a modernized layout, and streamlined navigation for a more visually rich and seamless user experience. Key improvements include an updated interface with simplified menu buttons, a new "shopping view" without sidebar ads, and centralized delivery options. The company plans to continue refining the search experience using AI and machine learning to offer relevant information and foster a more engaging environment for users.

title: How eBay uses generative AI to make employees and online sellers more productive
summary: eBay is leveraging generative AI to increase productivity for employees and enhance the online shopping experience for sellers and buyers. CTO Mazen Rawashdeh emphasized that AI is a tool to improve job satisfaction and development opportunities for engineers, aiming for a 15-20% productivity boost within two years. The company has developed eBay Coder, an LLM trained on its own code, and introduced features like "magical listing" and AI-generated image backdrops to assist sellers, while also using AI for customer service and gleaning insights from vast customer data.

title: Nine-figure podcast deals are back. Will it go any better this time?
summary: SiriusXM is investing heavily in podcasting, signing nine-figure deals similar to Spotify's past spending spree, but with a different strategy. Unlike Spotify's initial exclusive-platform approach, SiriusXM is focusing on acquiring advertising and distribution rights across multiple platforms and offering premium content through subscriptions. This new model aims for better economies of scale by reaching a broader audience, which could be a more sustainable approach than previous exclusivity attempts.

title: Magistrate Judge Recommends $54 Million in Damages Against Defaulting Defendants in Abbott Labs Diabetes Test Strips Case
summary: A magistrate judge in the Eastern District of New York has recommended a default judgment and permanent injunction against 85 defendants in a lawsuit filed by Abbott Laboratories. The recommendation includes over $54 million in enhanced damages for Abbott due to the unauthorized sale of international versions of its FreeStyle Diabetes test strips in the U.S., which led to consumer confusion and loss of goodwill. Despite the finding of willful infringement, the judge did not recommend prejudgment interest.

title: Side Hustles on eBay Gain Popularity in Japan as Inflation Bites
summary: As inflation rises in Japan, many individuals are turning to side hustles, particularly selling goods on eBay, to supplement their income. One notable example is Danny Chou, who quit his day job to focus on selling Japanese vintage record players and game consoles to international buyers, earning a significant amount monthly. This trend highlights how changing economic conditions are encouraging new forms of entrepreneurship.

title: EBay wants newsletter publishers its employees harassed to ID sources
summary: EBay has asked a judge to compel newsletter publishers David and Ina Steiner to reveal their news sources to help the e-commerce company defend against a lawsuit filed by the couple. EBay argues that the information is necessary to address the Steiners' claim of over $12 million in economic damages due to harassment affecting their ability to report. Seven former eBay employees have pleaded guilty to harassing the Steiners, who claim the harassment stifled their reporting and scared off potential sources, a claim EBay wants to investigate by identifying those sources.

title: eBay Publishes New Report on European Union Small Business Exporting
summary: eBay has published a new report highlighting that 97% of its small businesses in the EU are exporters, significantly surpassing the 4.8% average for traditional EU businesses. The report emphasizes how eBay's global marketplace helps SMEs achieve digitalization and fosters international trade, contributing to their resilience and growth across a wide range of markets and regions. This success supports inclusive opportunities and sustainable economic growth throughout Europe.

title: eBay Appoints CarMax CEO Bill Nash To Board Of Directors
summary: eBay has appointed CarMax CEO Bill Nash to its Board of Directors, effective September 18, 2024, aiming to leverage his retail and e-commerce expertise to drive business strategy and achieve sustainable long-term growth. Nash expressed enthusiasm for contributing to eBay's future in e-commerce and increasing shareholder value. This appointment comes as eBay faces various challenges, including stalled growth, regulatory scrutinies, and ongoing efforts to refine its business strategy.

title: eBay appoints CarMax CEO Bill Nash to its board
summary: eBay Inc. has appointed William (Bill) D. Nash, CEO of CarMax, as an independent director to its board, effective Wednesday. Nash brings extensive experience in retail and e-commerce, having led CarMax's technological transformation and omnichannel retailing efforts. This strategic move aims to enhance eBay's focus on sustainable, long-term growth and shareholder value.

title: CarMax CEO William Nash joins eBay board of directors
summary: eBay Inc. has appointed William D. Nash, President and CEO of CarMax, to its board of directors, effective September 18, 2024. Nash brings extensive experience in retail and e-commerce, building customer loyalty, and growing businesses. His expertise is expected to help eBay further its strategy and focus on sustainable, long-term growth.

title: CarMax CEO William Nash joins eBay board of directors
summary: eBay Inc. has appointed William (Bill) D. Nash, President and CEO of CarMax, to its board of directors, effective September 18, 2024. Nash brings extensive experience in retail and e-commerce, including building customer loyalty and driving business growth at CarMax. His expertise is expected to help eBay further its strategy and focus on sustainable, long-term growth.

title: eBay Welcomes Samantha Wellington As Chief Legal Officer
summary: eBay has appointed Samantha Wellington as its new Chief Legal Officer, effective October 7, 2024. She will report directly to CEO Jamie Iannone and oversee all global legal aspects of the company. Her appointment comes as eBay navigates significant legal challenges, including ongoing litigation related to a cyberstalking scandal, the deferred prosecution agreement with the DOJ, and regulatory scrutiny over its TCGPlayer acquisition.

title: Renault and Dacia launch official eBay stores
summary: eBay has launched official stores for Renault and Dacia, making genuine parts and accessories available for both new and older models. This partnership aims to simplify vehicle maintenance by providing thousands of high-quality components backed by eBay Assured Fit and the My Garage feature. Renault and Dacia join other major manufacturers on eBay, solidifying its position as a leading marketplace for authentic automotive parts.

title: eBay Announces Appointment of Gigi Ganatra Duff as Chief Communications Officer
summary: eBay Inc. has appointed Gigi Ganatra Duff as its new Chief Communications Officer, effective September 23, 2024. She will lead global communications, including internal, external, social media, public affairs, and ESG communications, reporting to Chief Growth Officer Julie Loeger. Duff brings over two decades of experience, including seven years as VP of Corporate Affairs at Nordstrom, and is expected to drive transformative change and strengthen eBay's brand and social impact initiatives.

title: Gigi Ganatra Duff to Head Up eBay’s Global Communications
summary: Gigi Ganatra Duff has been appointed as eBay Inc.'s chief communications officer, overseeing global communications, social media, public affairs, and ESG communications. She previously held executive roles at Nordstrom, Lionheart Capital, HSN, and Starwood Hotels. Her appointment comes as eBay aims to reinvent the future of e-commerce, with Duff emphasizing the company's mission to create economic opportunity and amplify its community stories.

title: Nordstrom veteran joins eBay as chief comms officer
summary: Gigi Ganatra Duff, a Nordstrom veteran, has been appointed as eBay's new chief communications officer. Reporting to Chief Growth Officer Julie Loeger, Duff will lead global communications, including internal, external, social media, public affairs, and ESG communications. Her extensive experience in corporate affairs and communications is expected to bring fresh energy and innovation to eBay during its current transformation.

title: eBay Foundation Awards $3M to Help Drive Inclusive Entrepreneurship
summary: The eBay Foundation has announced its 2024 Global Give grantees, awarding nearly $3 million to 31 nonprofit organizations worldwide. This initiative aims to foster inclusive entrepreneurship by removing barriers for historically excluded groups. Key grant recipients include Defy Ventures, Global Sisters, and Hatch Enterprise, which support formerly incarcerated individuals, women, and underrepresented entrepreneurs, respectively.

title: eBay Open 2024 Day 2: Keynote & A Big Announcement For 30th Anniversary Event In 2025
summary: eBay Open 2024 Day 2 included updates on marketing efforts, an expanded partnership with Liberis for Flexible Cash Advance financing for sellers, and advertising product discussions. A significant announcement revealed plans for a large, in-person eBay Open event in 2025 to celebrate the company's 30th anniversary, alongside a virtual option. The keynote also touched on improvements to the VeRO program and a critique of eBay's evasive responses regarding seller feedback issues.

title: EBay wins dismissal of US lawsuit over sale of harmful products
summary: A federal judge has dismissed a U.S. Department of Justice lawsuit against eBay, ruling that Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act shields the online platform from liability for harmful products sold by users. The lawsuit had accused eBay of allowing the sale of hundreds of thousands of illegal items, including pesticides and devices that evade vehicle emissions controls. The judge determined that eBay's administrative support did not make it a "publisher or speaker" for sellers, nor a "seller" of the products itself.

title: eBay removes fees for private sellers in the UK as competition hots up
summary: eBay Inc. has eliminated selling fees for private sellers in the UK across most categories to better compete with rivals like Depop and Vinted. This strategic move, following a successful fee removal for pre-owned clothing, aims to increase listings, promote sustainability, and capitalize on the significant potential for resale income from unused household items in the UK. The decision is effective immediately and removes final value and regulatory fees.

title: Meet Three Women Who Are Redefining Sports Card Collecting
summary: This article introduces three women—Anna Kim, La Tonya Hampton, and Alex McFarlane—who are challenging the traditional male-dominated landscape of sports card collecting. They share their unique journeys into the hobby, emphasizing their passion for sports, their strategic use of platforms like eBay for buying and selling, and their experiences as women in a growing community. Their stories highlight the increasing diversity and evolving nature of sports card collecting.

title: Poolside raises $500M to take on GitHub and others with its AI coding assistants
summary: Poolside Inc., an AI coding startup, has secured $500 million in a Series B funding round, bringing its total raised capital to $626 million and valuing the company at $3 billion. The company, co-founded by former GitHub CTO Jason Warner, is developing generative AI coding assistants that utilize reinforcement learning to continuously improve, aiming to compete with existing solutions like GitHub Copilot. Poolside plans to use the new funds for R&D, go-to-market initiatives, and purchasing 10,000 Nvidia GPUs to train future models, emphasizing its ambition to achieve artificial general intelligence in software development.

title: Google, Intuit executives switch industries to join real estate platform Opendoor
summary: Opendoor Technologies, a residential real estate e-commerce company, has appointed Selim Freiha, formerly of Google, as its new Chief Financial Officer and Shrisha Radhakrishna, previously from Intuit and LegalZoom, as Chief Technology and Product Officer. Both executives are set to join in November, bringing their tech industry experience to help Opendoor drive growth, enhance financial performance, and improve customer-centric digital solutions for real estate transactions.

title: CFOs On the Move: Week ending Oct. 4
summary: This article details significant CFO appointments and departures across various companies for the week ending October 4. Key moves include Michelle Chang becoming Zoom's CFO, Selim Freiha joining Opendoor, and Sheamus Toal stepping down from The Children's Place. Other appointments include Marcus Sanford at PrizePicks, Sandra Harris at Genesco, Tim Bosslet at Chomps, John Townsend at Ribbon Communications, and Siddharth Hariharan at Vast.

title: Pitney Bowes launches e-commerce shipping platform
summary: Pitney Bowes has introduced ShipAccel, a new e-commerce shipping platform designed to enhance shipping operations for businesses. The platform offers discounted rates from major carriers, integrates with popular e-commerce marketplaces, and allows for carrier performance evaluation, rate comparison, and branded tracking. Pitney Bowes aims to differentiate ShipAccel through its scalability, catering to businesses from small shippers to large-volume operations, and plans to introduce new features like cartonization.

title: eBay expands Circular Fashion Fund to start-ups, delivering $1.2 million investment by the end of 2025
summary: eBay announced the global expansion of its Circular Fashion Fund (CFF), committing $1.2 million in investment by the end of 2025 to support fashion start-ups focused on circular solutions. The CFF will also provide over 200 hours of mentoring and networking support, with one global winner receiving a $300,000 investment as "Circular Fashion Innovator of the Year." The fund aims to scale circular solutions from production to end-of-life, including rental and repair services, and has partnered with fashion councils in the UK, US, Germany, and Australia.

title: Expedia Group fills vacant CTO role with former Fanatics exec
summary: Expedia Group has appointed Ramana Thumu as its new chief technology officer, filling a role that had been vacant for five months since the dismissal of the previous CTO due to a policy violation. Thumu joins Expedia after a decade at Fanatics Commerce and ten years at eBay, bringing extensive experience in building and managing multi-tenant tech platforms. His appointment comes as Expedia is heavily investing in technology, including generative AI, loyalty programs, and expanded B2B offerings.

title: eBay (EBAY) Earnings: 3Q24 Key Numbers
summary: eBay Inc. reported its third-quarter 2024 earnings, with revenue up 3% to $2.6 billion and net income at $634 million. Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) increased by 2% to $18.3 billion. The company provided full-year 2024 guidance, expecting revenue between $10.23-10.29 billion and adjusted EPS of $4.80-4.85.

title: EBay Stock Sinks on Concerns About Holiday Shopping Season
summary: EBay's stock tumbled after the company provided weaker-than-expected guidance for the crucial holiday shopping season, despite beating profit, sales, and gross merchandise volume estimates in the third quarter. Analysts from Jefferies and Deutsche Bank expressed concerns about the durability of eBay's growth, particularly regarding its gross merchandise volume outlook for the fourth quarter. The company's shares dropped 8% in late-morning trading, though they have still gained about a third of their value this year.

title: Victims of eBay harassment campaign can seek punitive damages, US judge rules
summary: A federal judge ruled that a Massachusetts couple, David and Ina Steiner, can seek punitive damages against eBay for a harassment campaign perpetrated by eBay employees. While some claims related to physical actions in Massachusetts won't allow punitive damages under state law, other claims concerning online conduct from eBay's California headquarters will permit punitive damages under California law. This decision clarifies a legal question that was a sticking point in settlement talks, with the Steiners already seeking over $12 million in economic damages.

title: eBay Taps Nordstrom, Mattel Alum Alexis Hoopes To Lead Global Fashion Initiatives
summary: eBay has appointed Alexis Hoopes, formerly of Nordstrom and Mattel, as its new VP Global Head of Fashion, signaling a strategic shift towards consumer-to-consumer (C2C) selling and pre-loved fashion. This comes as eBay navigates significant changes, including the departure of previous fashion leadership, a shift in business development strategy, and increasing competition from other marketplaces and fee structure challenges in its UK market. Hoopes is expected to leverage her experience to expand eBay's global fashion initiatives, focusing on AI authentication tools and circular fashion, while addressing competitive pressures and investor concerns about revenue guidance.

title: eBay Is Overvalued Amid Guidance Overshadowing Its Q3 Positives (EBAY)
summary: A 10-year EV-to-FCF-multiple-adjusted DCF model suggests eBay stock is nearly 2% overvalued. While Q3 results were strong, Q4 and full-year 2024 guidance fell short of consensus estimates, leading to a Hold rating. The author believes EBAY is unlikely to deliver significant alpha unless management prioritizes dividend yield.

title: eBay for Charity Co-hosts 8th Celebrity Auction to Build Adapted Homes for Injured Veterans
summary: eBay for Charity and Homes For Our Troops (HFOT) are collaborating for their eighth annual celebrity auction to raise funds for custom adapted homes for severely injured veterans. The auction, running from November 11-21, 2024, features VIP experiences and memorabilia from celebrities like Jason Bateman, Jerry Seinfeld, and Will Ferrell. All proceeds directly support HFOT's mission of providing independence and stability for post-9/11 veterans.

title: Exploring high growth characteristics of META PLATFORMS INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:META).
summary: This article identifies META Platforms Inc-Class A (NASDAQ:META) as a potential breakout candidate due to its high technical and setup ratings from ChartMill. It highlights META's strong performance, positive financial metrics like increased quarterly earnings, revenue growth, and robust operating margins, alongside a conservative debt-to-equity ratio and consistent EPS beats. The analysis suggests strong prospects for further price appreciation based on both technical and fundamental indicators.

title: eBay Evo: Does New Modern, Simple Design Still Deliver Experience Users Need?
summary: eBay is launching "Evo," a new brand design system aimed at modernization and simplification to attract younger shoppers. While eBay touts Evo as enhancing the customer experience and humanizing interactions, the article questions if it truly delivers the essential functionality required by long-time buyers and sellers. It highlights concerns about irrelevant AI results, "off-brand" graphics, technical glitches in supposedly simplified seller tools, and continued ad-clutter on item pages, contradicting promises of improved user experience.

title: One of the World’s Fastest Race Car Drivers is a Top eBay Motors Seller
summary: Mark Rau, a world record-holding race car driver, has successfully transformed his passion for racing into a multimillion-dollar high-performance auto salvage business on eBay Motors. Starting from a two-car garage, AZ Cycle Parts now operates a 40,000-square-foot facility, specializing in hard-to-find luxury and enthusiast car parts. Rau credits eBay and its tools, like myFitment and International Shipping, for enabling his business growth and global reach, emphasizing the importance of hard work and reputation for aspiring sellers.

title: Tesla robot toy sells out, prompting eBay frenzy
summary: The $40 Tesla Bot Action Figure, standing 7 inches tall with 20 points of articulation, quickly sold out upon release. This rapid sellout has led to a frenzy on eBay, where the robot toys are now being resold for hundreds of dollars. The high demand for this collectible item highlights the strong market for Tesla-branded merchandise.

title: Can Xockets Enjoin NVIDIA and Microsoft Post eBay?
summary: Xockets, Inc. has filed a complaint against NVIDIA, Microsoft, and RPX Corporation for alleged patent infringement and antitrust violations, seeking an injunction in addition to monetary damages. The case explores the challenges of obtaining injunctive relief in patent cases post-eBay, where courts apply a stricter four-factor test and consider additional criteria. The article also discusses potential legislative changes, such as the RESTORE Patent Rights Act, aimed at strengthening patent owners' ability to secure injunctions.

title: Living Our Values Assessment Update
summary: eBay has completed an independent third-party assessment of its values and Human Rights Policy Statement, prompted by investor feedback. The assessment affirmed eBay's commitment to employee expression, non-interference with union choices, and good-faith bargaining. eBay is now implementing recommendations to enhance the implementation and awareness of its Human Rights Policy Statement, including establishing a cross-functional review team.

title: This eBay for Charity Seller Transforms Lives, One Meal at a Time
summary: Laurie Wong uses her eBay for Charity store, Reflections of Trinity, to fund a significant food pantry and resource center in Powder Springs, Georgia. Her efforts, stemming from a childhood spent in a convent soup kitchen, now feed thousands each month and provide support for seniors, students, and struggling families. The article highlights how eBay has enabled her small nonprofit to grow into a global business, expanding its reach and impact.

title: #NotAloneChallenge Auction Launches to Support Youth Mental Health
summary: eBay for Charity has partnered with the Inspiring Children Foundation (ICF) to launch the #NotAloneChallenge Auction. This 10-day online auction features exclusive experiences with figures like Steve Wozniak and David Solomon, with all proceeds benefiting ICF's programs that address youth mental health challenges. The initiative aims to democratize mental health support and tools, building on ICF's successful 25-year track record of empowering at-risk youth.

title: eBay recognises top sellers for global exporting excellence at Exporter of the Year event
summary: eBay hosted its annual Exporter of the Year 2024 event in Bangkok, Thailand, recognizing 24 top sellers from Global Emerging Market regions for their outstanding growth and buyer experience. The event highlighted the entrepreneurial spirit and global exporting excellence of these sellers, particularly those from Southeast Asia excelling in categories like Parts & Accessories and Watches. eBay affirmed its commitment to supporting sellers in cross-border trade, enabling them to expand their operations internationally and foster a vibrant global community.

title: Rent the Runway slips after another unfashionable quarter
summary: Rent the Runway's stock dropped over 20% after reporting earnings that, while in line with estimates, highlighted its flat revenue since 2022 and continued unprofitability since its 2009 founding. The company's struggles are contrasted with Urban Outfitters' profitable competitor, Nuuly, though Rent the Runway is losing less money than it did in previous years.

title: Unpacked: How brands are complying with California’s new baby food transparency law
summary: A new California law (AB 899) going into effect in January 2025 will require baby food companies to test for and disclose heavy metal levels in their products. This regulation aims to increase transparency for consumers, building on previous concerns about heavy metal contamination. Brands are carefully developing strategies, such as using QR codes linking to detailed testing results, to comply with the law and educate consumers without causing undue alarm.

title: Tony Coccerino, Nuuly | Modern Retail Vanguard 2024
summary: Tony Coccerino, Nuuly's director of fulfillment, is highlighted in Modern Retail's Vanguard series for his crucial role in the fashion rental service's success. Since joining in 2020, he has overseen Nuuly's two facilities, managing the complex process of returns, laundering, and inspections for 300,000 subscribers. Nuuly, owned by Urban Outfitters, Inc., recently achieved its most profitable quarter, with revenue growing to $97 million, a 48% year-over-year increase, underscoring the effectiveness of Coccerino's operational strategies and the company's commitment to efficiency and quality control.

title: eBay and Klarna Expand Partnership, Bringing Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) to New Markets
summary: eBay and Klarna have expanded their strategic partnership to offer Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) options to shoppers in key European markets, including the U.K., Austria, France, Italy, the Netherlands, and Spain. This expansion provides greater payment flexibility for eBay buyers and introduces a new feature allowing Klarna users to easily resell items bought through the app on eBay. The initiative aims to enhance affordability, support the growing resale market, and attract a younger demographic to eBay.

title: 3 Reasons Why Tractor Supply Is The Next Decade’s Retailer To Watch
summary: Tractor Supply Company plans significant expansion, aiming for 3,200 stores and growing its Petsense by Tractor Supply banner. This growth is anticipated to make it a major national retailer, leveraging its scale for increased retail media revenue and positioning it as a key player in the pet care market. The company projects substantial increases in total addressable market, net sales, comparable store sales, and EPS, potentially moving it into the top 25 U.S. retailers by 2030.

title: The RESTORE Patent Rights Act: One Little Sentence that Could Change Everything
summary: The RESTORE Patent Rights Act, a one-sentence bill, proposes to create a rebuttable presumption for permanent injunctions in patent infringement cases, partially reversing the 2006 eBay Inc. v. MercExchange ruling. A Senate Judiciary Committee hearing revealed deep divisions, with supporters arguing it prevents "efficient infringement" and critics warning it could benefit "patent trolls" and stifle innovation. The debate centers on balancing patent holders' exclusive rights with broader economic and social impacts, questioning whether the U.S. patent system should return to pre-eBay practices or adopt more flexible international models.

title: Tech Moves: Expedia names CFO; former Microsoft AI VP leads new startup; Avante gets sales VP
summary: Expedia Group appoints Scott Schenkel as its new CFO, while former Microsoft AI VP Luis Vargas leaves to launch an unnamed AI startup. Seattle healthcare startup Avante hires Jonathan Smith as EVP of commercial growth and sales. The article also covers other significant personnel changes across various tech companies and organizations in the Pacific Northwest.

title: Recognizing our Top Sellers of 2024
summary: eBay celebrated its top sellers of 2024 through various events globally, recognizing their outstanding achievements and contributions to the marketplace. The article highlights winners from Dubai, Lithuania, Singapore, Germany, Japan, UK, and China, showcasing their entrepreneurial spirit and business success on eBay. These sellers represent diverse categories, from luxury resale and electronics refurbishment to sports trading cards and auto parts.

title: Expedia names former eBay exec Scott Schenkel as CFO
summary: Expedia has appointed Scott Schenkel, formerly of eBay and GE, as its new Chief Financial Officer. He will be replacing Julie Whalen, who is scheduled to remain with the company through the end of February.

title: eBay Loses Motors Events Manager, NASCAR Driver Sarah Burgess To Walmart In Race For Enthusiast Buyers
summary: Walmart continues its strategy of challenging eBay in key focus categories by attracting talent, with Sarah Burgess, eBay's Sr Partnership US Motors Events Manager and NASCAR driver, moving to Walmart Marketplace. Burgess will take on a Senior Management Business Development role in Parts and Accessories, a critical category for eBay. This move highlights Walmart's aggressive push into enthusiast buyer verticals, including collectibles and pre-owned goods, often by hiring former eBay executives.

title: eBay Acquires Car Auction Site Caramel To Streamline & Simplify The Vehicle Buying Experience
summary: eBay has acquired Caramel, an online automotive transaction platform, to enhance its eBay Motors division. This acquisition aims to streamline and simplify the vehicle buying experience by handling complexities like paperwork, financing, and transportation. The move signifies eBay's strategy to build trust for vehicle buyers and integrates Caramel's end-to-end service, which currently involves Caramel acting as a middleman to protect both buyers and sellers.

title: eBay Sellers Say Sales At A Standstill With Traffic Reports Lagging, Sign Out Errors & Other Technical Issues 1-13-25
summary: eBay sellers are reporting widespread technical issues, including zero organic views in their traffic reports and repeated sign-out errors when trying to list items. While an eBay community representative confirmed the traffic report issue as sitewide, sellers are frustrated by the lack of transparency and suspect that these delays might be intentional load-balancing prioritizing buyer experience over seller functionality. This comes after previous issues with eBay's new Traffic Report experience.

title: Got a side hustle? Watch out for this tax change
summary: A significant tax change affecting side hustlers and those earning income through third-party payment apps like Venmo, Etsy, and eBay is now in effect. If you earned over $5,000 through these apps in 2024 (dropping to $2,500 next year), you'll receive a 1099-K form. The article advises keeping detailed records and distinguishing between personal reimbursements and business income to ensure correct tax reporting.

title: eBay expands online car sales business by buying app
summary: eBay has acquired the automotive mobile app Caramel, expanding its online car sales business. This acquisition comes shortly after the e-commerce giant was fined $3 million by the Justice Department. The move is seen as positive news for the company following recent legal troubles.

title: NASDAQ:META, a strong growth stock, setting up for a breakout.
summary: META PLATFORMS INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:META) has been identified as a strong growth stock with positive indicators across growth, health, and profitability metrics. ChartMill's analysis highlights impressive historical and projected EPS and revenue growth, robust financial health with low debt, and excellent profitability ratios compared to industry peers. The stock also shows a favorable setup pattern with consolidation and reduced volatility, suggesting potential for a breakout.

title: eBay & Ex-Execs Make Last-Ditch Effort To Escape Liability For 2019 Cyberstalking Scandal
summary: eBay and its former executives, Devin Wenig, Steve Wymer, and Wendy Jones, are attempting to avoid liability in the 2019 cyberstalking scandal by filing motions for summary judgment to dismiss claims against them. The civil lawsuit, filed by victims Ina and David Steiner, seeks $500 million in damages after the company paid a $3 million fine to the US Treasury, not the victims, as part of a deferred prosecution agreement. The defense argues that statements linking executives to the criminal activity are inadmissible hearsay and that their communications were taken out of context or referred to legal strategies, not criminal encouragement.

title: Top 10 Tech Companies to Work for in San Jose in 2025
summary: San Jose is identified as a leading tech hub in 2025, boasting over 6,600 tech companies and an annual tech GDP of $275 billion, with competitive salaries ranging from $140,000 to $200,000. The article highlights top employers like Adobe, Cisco, and Zoom, emphasizing their innovation in AI, green technology, and cybersecurity, alongside strong company cultures. It details how these companies foster career growth, employee satisfaction, and significant industry impact, making San Jose an attractive destination for tech professionals.

title: eBay Buys Valley-based Auto Firm
summary: eBay Inc. has acquired Encino-based Caramel, an auto transaction solution provider, to enhance its U.S. vehicles category with "innovative technology and seamless transaction tools." Caramel, founded in 2021, specializes in streamlining online car transactions, including identity verification, financing, and insurance. The acquisition aims to offer eBay users a safe and streamlined way to purchase vehicles while expanding access to hassle-free vehicle transactions.

title: Expedia confirms layoffs at Seattle travel giant
summary: Expedia Group has confirmed further layoffs as part of its ongoing efforts to cut costs and improve efficiencies. While the company did not disclose specific numbers or roles, reports suggest marketing and creative teams were impacted. These layoffs follow a reduction of 1,500 roles in the previous year and occur as Expedia focuses on expanding profit margins and reinvesting in strategic areas after reporting strong fourth-quarter revenue growth.

title: eBay Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
summary: eBay Inc. reported its fourth quarter and full year 2024 results, highlighting that revenue reached $2.6 billion for Q4 and $10.3 billion for the full year, both up 1% and 2% respectively on an as-reported and FX-Neutral basis. The company also announced Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) of $19.3 billion for Q4 and $74.7 billion for the full year, showing growth of 4% and 2% on an as-reported basis. eBay returned $1.0 billion to stockholders in Q4, including share repurchases and cash dividends, and provided first quarter 2025 financial guidance.

title: eBay Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
summary: eBay Inc. announced its fourth quarter and full year 2024 financial results, reporting a 1% revenue increase to $2.6 billion and a 4% rise in Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) to $19.3 billion for Q4. For the full year, revenue reached $10.3 billion with GMV at $74.7 billion, both up 2% on an as-reported basis. The company returned $1.0 billion to stockholders in Q4, including $900 million in share repurchases and $128 million in cash dividends, and provided first-quarter 2025 guidance.

title: EBAY INC SEC 10-K Report
summary: eBay Inc. has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, highlighting significant financial growth with net revenues of $10,283 million and an operating income of $2,318 million. The report details strategic initiatives such as expanding advertising solutions, implementing AI-driven features, and emphasizing sustainability through recommerce. eBay also addressed challenges including intense competition, foreign currency fluctuations, and cybersecurity risks, outlining plans to leverage technology and manage capital effectively for future growth.

title: DHL Express Japan Helps eBay Cut Scope 3 GHG Emissions Using Sustainable Aviation Fuel
summary: DHL Express Japan and eBay Inc. have signed a GoGreen Plus agreement, effective January 2025, to reduce eBay's Scope 3 greenhouse gas emissions from international express shipments through the use of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). Orange Connex, an official eBay logistics partner, purchased nearly 500 metric tons of SAF credits to support this initiative, particularly for the "eBay SpeedPAK – Ship via DHL" service in Japan. This partnership underscores eBay's commitment to decarbonization, aiming for a 27.5% reduction in transportation-related emissions by 2030, and aligns with DHL Express's goal of achieving net-zero logistics-related GHG emissions by 2050.

title: eBay (NASDAQ:EBAY) Is Increasing Its Dividend To $0.29
summary: eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY) has announced an increase in its dividend to $0.29, payable on March 28th. This raises the annual payment to 1.8% of the current stock price, which is consistent with the industry average. The dividend is projected to be well-covered by earnings and cash flow, with a forecast payout ratio of 26% after an expected 18.2% rise in earnings per share over the next year.

title: eBay for Charity Raised Over $192 Million in 2024
summary: eBay for Charity raised over $192.5 million for non-profits globally in 2024, marking an 18% year-over-year increase and representing a record-shattering year. Key initiatives included a "Forces For Change" auction with British Vogue, the historic "Power of One Charity Auction Lunch" with Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff, and Elton John's "Rocket Man Resale." The platform also highlighted seller Laurie Wong, whose nonprofit thrift store on eBay fuels a food pantry and community programs.

title: JD Earnings: JD.Com reports higher Q4 2024 revenue and adj. profit
summary: JD.com Inc. reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024, with a double-digit increase in both revenue and adjusted net income. Adjusted earnings per ADS rose by 40% year-over-year to $1.02, while unadjusted net profit more than doubled to $1.4 billion. The company's revenues grew by 13% annually to $47.5 billion, driven by increases in both net product and net service revenue.

title: Costco Wholesale Becomes Oversold (COST)
summary: Costco Wholesale Corp (COST) shares have entered oversold territory with an RSI reading of 28.4. This technical indicator suggests that the stock has experienced heavy selling, potentially signaling an upcoming buying opportunity for bullish investors. The article highlights Costco's current stock price relative to its 52-week range and advises considering the RSI as a measure of market sentiment.

title: E-Commerce Automotive Aftermarket Size, Share | CAGR of 22.4%
summary: The global E-Commerce Automotive Aftermarket is projected to grow from USD 67.4 Billion in 2024 to USD 508.7 Billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 22.4%. This growth is driven by digitalization, increasing vehicle ownership, and consumer preference for online shopping, with North America currently dominating the market. Key trends include AI-powered recommendations, mobile-friendly platforms, omnichannel strategies, and the emergence of eco-friendly auto parts.

title: eBay Privacy Policy April 2025: Blockchain & AI Tech Enable Digital Authentication & More
summary: eBay's updated privacy policy, effective April 21, 2025, foreshadows significant integrations of blockchain and AI technologies. The changes hint at new digital authentication certificates leveraging blockchain for luxury items and outline eBay's commitment to responsible AI, including its use in listing generation, fraud detection, and customer service. The policy also details how user data may be used for AI training, with an opt-out option available, though it's unclear if this applies to US users.

title: eBay Foundation Board Taps Three New Leaders to Drive Impact
summary: The eBay Foundation has appointed Alex Kazim, Julie Cheng, and John Feldmeier to its Board of Directors, bringing diverse strategic expertise to further its mission of fostering inclusive economic growth. These new members, starting their terms on February 27, will help the Foundation continue its work in empowering communities and removing barriers to entrepreneurship for historically excluded groups. The Foundation has granted nearly $18 million in 2024 and over $139 million since 1998, supporting more than 1,800 nonprofits.

title: Palmetto Grain Brokerage -
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) is outperforming the Nasdaq, with its shares up 5.1% over the past three months compared to the Nasdaq Composite's 8.4% decline, and 29.7% over the past year. Despite beating Q4 earnings estimates, the stock dropped due to underwhelming Q1 revenue guidance. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with a modest upside potential from current levels.

title: Could Recession Pull EBAY Stock Down To $20?
summary: The article discusses the potential vulnerability of eBay (EBAY) stock to a recession, suggesting it could fall to $20 based on historical performance during economic downturns. Despite eBay's recent outperformance against the S&P 500, broader market concerns, including tariffs and declining consumer confidence, raise risks. The piece advises investors to consider macroeconomic indicators and eBay's past volatility when assessing its future prospects.

title: Brands Briefing: FP Movement’s growth playbook for 2025
summary: This Brands Briefing focuses on FP Movement's 2025 growth strategy, which includes expanding its physical store presence and community-building events to differentiate itself in the competitive activewear market. The article also covers 1-800 Contacts' new glasses brand, The Framery, and Faherty's launch of a print catalog called "The Faherty Chronicles" to build its brand lifestyle.

title: Spring clean your way to £1K: eBay and Oti Mabuse launch clearout challenge
summary: eBay and Oti Mabuse have launched the 'List to £1K' challenge to encourage people to sell unwanted items and earn money, with a focus on spring cleaning. New research reveals that while 75% of Brits plan to spring clean, many are hesitant, leading to unused items accumulating in homes. The challenge aims to simplify the process through weekly steps and leveraging eBay's features, with participants potentially earning up to £1,000.

title: eBay backs WunderGraph to build an open source GraphQL federation
summary: WunderGraph, a fledgling open-source startup focused on GraphQL federation, has secured $7.5 million in Series A funding from investors including eBay Ventures, Karma Ventures, and Aspenwood Ventures. eBay is also partnering with WunderGraph as a core design partner to build an open-source alternative to proprietary GraphQL federation products like Apollo. This collaboration aims to enhance eBay's API ecosystem and enable larger enterprises to adopt open-source solutions for API management at scale.

title: eBay: Poised To Dominate Its e-Commerce Niche
summary: eBay is strategically focusing on the non-new, pre-owned item market, which differentiates it from large e-commerce competitors like Amazon and Alibaba. This niche focus allows eBay to leverage its profitability and scale against smaller, specialized competitors. The article suggests that eBay's lower valuation compared to e-commerce and retail leaders makes it an attractive investment opportunity.

title: eBay Finally Rolls Out Magical AI Listing Starting In Germany, Plus Updates On Facebook Marketplace Cross Posting
summary: eBay is beginning the rollout of its "Magical Listing" AI tool in Germany, allowing sellers to create listings simply by taking a picture, despite being delayed by over a year and a half. The company is also providing updates on its integration with Facebook Marketplace, which has faced challenges with listing visibility. This delayed AI tool places eBay behind competitors like Mercari, Depop, and Poshmark, all of whom have already launched similar features.

title: Salesforce Downsizes Bellevue Office Footprint
summary: Salesforce Inc. has reduced its office space in Bellevue, Washington, by renewing a lease for 98,000 square feet in the 929 Office Tower while simultaneously terminating an existing 78,000 square feet across three floors. The software giant has been an anchor tenant in the building since 2015. This move reflects a broader trend of companies, including Warner Bros. Discovery, downsizing their office footprints in the region.

title: Ecom Accelerator Expands Services to Include eBay Stores, Strengthening Long-Term Growth for Clients
summary: Ecom Accelerator has expanded its services to include eBay store management, in addition to its existing TikTok Shop offerings, to provide clients with a diversified and scalable approach to online selling. This strategy aims to combine TikTok Shop's early revenue potential with eBay's long-term growth opportunities. The company emphasizes a hands-off management model, providing comprehensive support from product selection to customer service for a resilient e-commerce business.

title: eBay invests $300k in Refiberd to drive circular fashion solutions
summary: eBay Ventures has invested $300,000 in Refiberd, naming it eBay's Circular Fashion Innovator of the Year, as part of its global Circular Fashion Fund. The fund aims to support startups that scale circular solutions in fashion, with a total investment of $1.2 million by the end of 2025. Refiberd's AI technology helps sort and recycle textile waste, promoting true circularity in the fashion industry.

title: eBay Cyberstalking Showdown: Suit Hangs On Ex-Security Director's Testimony & CIA Background
summary: The civil lawsuit stemming from the eBay cyberstalking scandal is at a critical juncture, with the testimony of former Senior Security Director Jim Baugh being central to the case. The defense team for eBay executives is attempting to lift a protective order to explore Baugh's alleged CIA background, arguing it's crucial for impeaching his credibility and for their defense. The judge has denied requests to lift the protective order, calling the information "marginally relevant" and untimely, while eBay itself has accused Baugh of attempting to "auction off" his testimony.

title: Criteo Announces Changes to its Board of Directors
summary: Criteo S.A. announced changes to its Board of Directors, naming Frederik van der Kooi as the new Chairperson, succeeding Rachel Picard who will remain a director. The company also nominated Stefanie Jay for election as a new independent director at its 2025 Annual General Meeting. Additionally, Hubert de Pesquidoux will not stand for re-election, which, with Jay’s election, will result in an eight-director board with seven independent members.

title: EBay says its new AI-aided tool cut steps to create listings in half
summary: EBay has implemented a new AI-powered "magical listing tool" that has reportedly reduced the steps required to create listings by 50%. The tool, which prioritizes simplification and mobile-friendliness, leverages AI to suggest product details after an image is uploaded, shifting from a text-based search approach. This initiative is part of eBay's broader strategy to integrate AI to improve seller efficiency and has already seen over 10 million sellers use existing AI features to generate billions in GMV.

title: Why Is eBay Stock Trading Higher On Wednesday?
summary: eBay Inc. stock rose after announcing a global payment partnership with Checkout.com to enhance the user experience and strengthen its digital payments infrastructure. This collaboration aims to prioritize security, speed, and ease of use in payment processing for eBay's massive global marketplace. The company's Vice President of Global Payments highlighted that this alliance aligns with eBay's vision for growth through financial services innovation.

title: eBay Achieves Sustainability Milestone A Year Ahead of Schedule
summary: eBay has reached its goal of powering all its operations with 100% renewable energy by 2024, a year ahead of its 2025 target. This achievement is part of broader Impact goals, including commitments to net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2045 and significant reductions in Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions by 2030, validated by the Science Based Targets initiative. The company also aims to create $22 billion in positive economic impact and avoid substantial carbon emissions and waste by 2025 through recommerce.

title: eBay and Klarna Deepen Global Strategic Partnership to Empower Flexible Shopping in the U.S.
summary: eBay and Klarna have expanded their global strategic partnership to the U.S. market, offering flexible payment options like "Pay in 4" and financing to millions of eBay shoppers. This collaboration aims to provide more choice, affordability, and control for buyers, particularly in categories like electronics, fashion, and collectibles. The partnership also promotes a circular economy through Klarna's resell feature, allowing users to easily list past purchases on eBay.

title: IBM Earnings: Highlights of IBM’s Q1 2025 report; earnings beat estimates
summary: International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) reported its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with revenues reaching $14.54 billion, an increase from $14.46 billion in Q1 2024. Adjusted earnings were $1.60 per share, surpassing estimates, despite being lower than the $1.68 per share reported a year prior. The company's unadjusted net income for the quarter was $1.06 billion or $1.12 per share.

title: DOJ Drops Appeal Of Section 230 Ruling In eBay EPA Case
summary: The US Department of Justice has dropped its appeal of a Section 230 dismissal in a case seeking to hold eBay liable for illegal items sold on its platform, allowing the previous ruling to stand. This decision is significant because it affirms Section 230 protections for online marketplaces, but also raises questions as eBay's subsidiary, TCGPlayer, resumed selling on the platform shortly after the appeal was dropped, contradicting eBay's previous defense of not being a direct seller. The article highlights the ongoing debate around Section 230 and its implications for accountability in online marketplaces, with other companies like Walmart also pursuing similar defenses against liability for illegal activities.

title: eBay Q1 2025 slides: Revenue and EPS exceed guidance as strategic initiatives gain traction
summary: eBay Inc. exceeded its first quarter 2025 financial guidance, reporting revenue of $2.58 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $1.38, both surpassing company expectations. The e-commerce giant saw growth in Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) and advertising revenue, driven by strategic initiatives in trading cards and fashion, as well as new AI-powered features for buyers and sellers. The company also returned $893 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, demonstrating strong financial health and a continued focus on operational efficiency.

title: eBay Announces CFO Transition and Executive Leadership Changes
summary: eBay announced that Chief Financial Officer, Steve Priest, will be leaving the company and Peggy Alford will take over as CFO effective May 12, 2025. In addition to the CFO change, eBay is restructuring its leadership to foster faster innovation and collaboration, consolidating product and market teams under Jordan Sweetnam as Chief Commercial Officer, and unifying engineering under Mazen Rawashdeh as Chief Technology Officer. These changes are aimed at driving long-term growth, enhancing customer experience, and integrating new technologies like AI.

title: Infographic: How eBay (EBAY) performed in Q1 2025
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) reported modest revenue growth in Q1 2025, reaching $2.6 billion, a 1% increase year-over-year on a reported basis. The company's net income rose to $503 million, with GAAP EPS at $1.06 and adjusted EPS at $1.38. For Q2 2025, eBay anticipates revenue between $2.59-2.66 billion and adjusted EPS in the range of $1.24-1.31.

title: eBay Appoints Brie Welch as Resident Stylist and Debuts 'eBay Watchlist' Trend Report
summary: eBay has named fashion stylist Brie Welch as its Resident Stylist and launched the "eBay Watchlist," a trend report based on the shopping behavior of its 134 million users. The report highlights the growing popularity of pre-loved fashion, with nearly 40% of clothing, shoes, and accessories sold on eBay in 2024 being pre-loved. Welch will identify trends and curate finds, emphasizing circularity in fashion, while the Watchlist identifies key trends like Modern Bohemia, Well Suited, Function First, Dyed in Dusk, and Blue Hues.

title: eBay Launches Auction of Pre-Loved Fashion and Custom Porsche Ahead of Miami Race Weekend
summary: eBay is hosting an exclusive auction featuring pre-loved luxury fashion and a customized 1993 Porsche 911 Carrera 4 Cabriolet, curated with model Winnie Harlow, ahead of Miami's race weekend. The auction highlights the fusion of fashion and motorsport culture, with all proceeds benefiting Vitiligo Support International. Bidding is open until May 12, offering unique finds and an interactive "Guaranteed Fit Stop" experience during the event.

title: Coursera and Canva registered as VAT payers in Uzbekistan
summary: Online education platform Coursera and graphic design service Canva have officially registered as VAT payers in Uzbekistan. This brings the total number of foreign legal entities registered for VAT to 69, with 11 companies registering this year alone, doubling the registrations from all of 2024. Tax revenue from foreign big tech companies in Q1 2025 increased by 50% compared to Q1 2024, reaching 36.2 billion UZS, primarily from Apple, Meta, Google, OpenAI, and TikTok.

title: EBay restructures leadership, brings in new CFO
summary: EBay announced a leadership restructuring, including the departure of CFO Steve Priest and Chief Product Officer Eddie Garcia. Former PayPal executive Peggy Alford will take over as CFO. The company is also consolidating its product and marketing teams and its engineering team, with Chief Commercial Officer Jordan Sweetnam and CTO Mazen Rawashdeh taking expanded roles. These changes and the new CFO come as eBay reported Q1 earnings with modest revenue and net income increases, alongside a "wider-than-usual" Q2 guidance due to tariff uncertainties.

title: eBay Launches 6th Annual Up & Running Grants Program, Awarding $500,000 to Small Businesses
summary: eBay has launched its sixth annual Up & Running Grants program, committing $500,000 to support small businesses. The initiative will provide 50 U.S. sellers with $10,000 grants, $500 eBay Refurbished credits, and access to coaching and resources to foster growth. Since its inception in 2020, the program has awarded over $2.5 million to 250 eBay sellers, helping them expand and invest in their businesses.

title: Disbanding The Group(M); TikTok Struts Its Stuff At NewFronts
summary: WPP is rebranding its ad-buying arm GroupM to WPP Media, aligning with similar moves by Publicis and Omnicom, amidst a "year of transition" for WPP following several account losses. Meanwhile, TikTok asserted its confidence in its platform's future at its NewFronts presentation, introducing new ad features and partnerships, despite ongoing regulatory uncertainties. Meta is also navigating a "trust me" phase as its Reality Labs expenditures and flat ad impression growth raise concerns, leading to a focus on cost management and potential shifts in strategy.

title: EBay launches resale trend report
summary: EBay has released its new "Watchlist" trend report, offering insights into shopper behavior and data within the pre-owned fashion market, drawing from its 134 million users. The report identified Louis Vuitton as the most-purchased designer label and Birkenstock as the fastest-growing brand on the platform in Q1 2025. EBay also announced the appointment of Brie Welch as its resident stylist to identify and comment on trends, further empowering the circular fashion economy.

title: Neil Sutton departs NCR Voyix to take on Chief Revenue Officer role at vision AI specialist Everseen
summary: Neil Sutton has left NCR Voyix to become the Chief Revenue Officer at Everseen, a company specializing in visual AI solutions for retailers. Sutton expressed his admiration for Everseen's founder and CEO, Alan O'Herlihy, and highlighted Everseen's position as a market leader in vision AI, trusted by top global retailers to reduce shrink and improve operations. The article also touches upon the RTIH AI in Retail Awards, which are open for entries and aim to celebrate innovation in AI within the retail sector.

title: Alphabet’s stock is now almost as cheap as eBay’s by this metric — but should you buy it?
summary: Alphabet's stock has become inexpensive based on its price-to-earnings multiple, making it appear nearly as cheap as eBay's, despite Alphabet being a tech juggernaut and investors having concerns about its slowing search-engine growth. The article questions whether this perceived cheapness is worth the risks, particularly given the strong bullish ratings from Wall Street analysts who have an average price target of $200.

title: How to Start an Etsy Shop in 10 Easy Steps
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide on how to open an Etsy shop and begin selling handmade crafts or vintage items online. It covers everything from setting up an account and choosing a shop name to photographing products, adding listings, selecting payment preferences, and understanding Etsy seller fees. The guide emphasizes the ease of setting up an online storefront on Etsy for new sellers.

title: eBay Publishes 2024 Impact Report
summary: eBay released its 2024 Impact Report, highlighting progress in economic empowerment, sustainable commerce, and workforce development. The report details eBay's contributions to small businesses, charitable giving, and efforts to reduce environmental impact through recommerce. It also emphasizes the company's commitment to a trusted marketplace and an empowered workforce.

title: eBay to close Syracuse office of TCGplayer, cutting about 220 jobs – Central New York Business Journal
summary: eBay announced plans to close its TCGplayer office in Syracuse, New York, resulting in approximately 220 job cuts. The operations will be moved to an eBay logistics center in Kentucky, a decision eBay states was made to consolidate operations and improve authentication capabilities. Syracuse City Auditor Alexander Marion criticized the move as "union busting" and called for a review of public benefits TCGplayer received from Empire State Development.

title: Is It Time To Consider Buying eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY)?
summary: eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY) has seen a significant price increase recently, yet its intrinsic value of $110.52 suggests it is still trading at a cheap price compared to the market. Although its expected earnings growth is a modest 3.8%, the current undervaluation, coupled with its high beta, may present a buying opportunity for investors. However, potential investors should also consider the company's capital structure and balance sheet strength before making an investment decision.

title: eBay Loses Compliance, M&A Leader As Molly Finn Leaves Legacy Of Legal & Regulatory Debacles Behind
summary: Molly Finn, eBay's former Chief Compliance Officer and Deputy General Counsel M&A and Securities, is departing the company after 17 years. Her tenure is marked by significant legal and regulatory issues, including the 2019 cyberstalking scandal and alleged due diligence failures concerning the TCGPlayer acquisition. Critics suggest her roles may have presented conflicts of interest, leading to compliance lapses and increased scrutiny for eBay.

title: EBAY Stock Has Huge, Unusual Call Option Volume - Highlighting Its Value
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) recently saw significant, unusual call option volume, especially at the $74.00 strike price for options expiring soon, suggesting strong institutional interest. The company's robust free cash flow generation, which increased 36% in Q1 and its FCF margin improved to almost 25%, indicates it is efficiently converting operations to cash. This financial strength suggests EBAY stock could be undervalued, with a potential price target of $87.87 per share, representing a 21.5% increase from its current trading price.

title: TCGPlayer workers file complaint with NLRB as eBay adds security
summary: Unionized TCGPlayer workers have filed a complaint with the National Labor Relations Board, alleging that parent company eBay is moving TCGPlayer's operations to Kentucky to avoid contract negotiations and engage in union-busting. Workers claim they were not properly notified of the closure, which will result in 220 job losses, and report increased security presence and harassment following the announcement. eBay disputes the union's claims, stating it informed CWA leadership and is investigating reports of security misconduct.

title: Ebay (EBAY): Company Profile, Stock Price, News, Rankings
summary: eBay operates online marketplaces globally, connecting buyers and sellers for a wide range of merchandise. Despite recent declines in active users, the company maintains strong performance in "focus categories" like trading cards and automobile parts. Reselling, or "re-commerce," accounts for a significant portion of its sales, emphasizing its role in sustainability.

title: Executives from Kroger, Publix, Sysco and Walmart Join GS1 US Board of Governors
summary: The GS1 US Board of Governors has welcomed four new industry leaders from Kroger, Publix, Sysco, and Walmart, while Brian Dick of Golden State Foods has been appointed vice chairman. These additions will help guide GS1 US in driving the adoption of GS1 Standards to enhance supply chain efficiency and visibility across various sectors. The executives bring extensive experience in supply chain planning, purchasing, national accounts, and strategic transformation, contributing valuable insights to improve industry operations and customer experiences.

title: eBay Launches Livestream Shopping Tour Across the U.S.
summary: eBay is launching "eBay Live on Tour," a live event series that will bring livestream shopping and collector-focused experiences to cities across the U.S. beginning June 14. The tour will feature top eBay sellers, curated inventory, special guests, and real-time broadcasts, allowing collectors to discover, shop, and connect both in person and online. This initiative aims to strengthen the community by meeting collectors where they are and celebrating the hobby.

title: eBay Goes On Livestream Shopping Tour In Search Of Elusive Enthusiast Buyers
summary: eBay is launching a new Livestream Shopping Tour across the U.S. with in-person and streaming events at hobby shops and conventions throughout 2025. This initiative is a rehash of previous pop-up strategies aimed at attracting "Enthusiast Buyers" and addressing slow adoption of eBay Live, despite facing significant competition and past technical issues with its livestream platform. The article also questions eBay's recent claim about eBay Live's international expansion to the UK, suggesting potential misrepresentation of timelines.

title: eBay Charges Sellers Ad Fees Months After Sales In Apparent Attribution Snafu
summary: eBay is reportedly charging sellers ad fees months after sales have completed, which the company claims is due to a "system error" that missed previous attribution for promoted listings. This issue has led to frustration among sellers, who are questioning eBay's transparency and demanding refunds, especially given past instances of similar technical issues with fee collection. Critics suggest the multi-billion dollar corporation should absorb these costs rather than penalize sellers, urging those affected to report the issue to the Federal Trade Commission.

title: eBay Spotlights the Past, Present, and Future of Women’s Basketball
summary: eBay is launching "Sue Bird’s Dream Draft," a curated collection of women's basketball memorabilia by WNBA legend Sue Bird. The collection, which includes trading cards and signed gear, celebrates both iconic and emerging players and runs from June 10 through June 17. This initiative highlights the significant surge in interest in women's basketball collectibles on eBay, driven by current stars and league pioneers, with strong market growth reflected in increased searches and sales.

title: TCGPlayer workers, eBay strike deal on severance package with company set to move
summary: Workers at TCGPlayer, unionized under the Communication Workers of America, have reached a severance agreement with eBay, the parent company, as TCGPlayer prepares to move its operations to Kentucky. The deal includes 20 weeks of pay, seven months of mental health benefits, and a ratification bonus for the 220 workers who will be laid off. This agreement represents a significant improvement over the initial offer, achieved after worker boycotts and community pressure.

title: EPA Drops Suit Against eBay
summary: The EPA has voluntarily dismissed its appeal against eBay, letting stand a district court's decision that eBay is not liable for third-party product sales violating environmental acts. The district court had ruled that eBay was not a seller, its involvement did not establish causation, and Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act protected it. Meanwhile, the government continues a similar appeal against EZ Lynk in the Second Circuit.

title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights: Tesla, eBay, PayPal and Alphabet
summary: Zacks Investment Ideas spotlights Tesla, eBay, PayPal, and Alphabet, focusing on Tesla's robotaxi debut. Tesla shares spiked over 6% after launching its robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, using modified Model Y vehicles.
